input:
Design Thinking for Innovation – Coursera Course by Luna Arias

Reflection – Learning Launch

Challenge    Last year I was part of a team that had the task of analyzing the causes that led to a  small club in my city not being able to add new members.  Initially we meet with various stakeholders to better understand the current situation.  Among them were partners, ex-partners, employees, parents of children who play  sports at the facilities, managers, fans, etc. From those conversations we obtained  preliminary information indicating that the activities the club had to offer were difficult to  search for. We were asked to create a new web system together with an app to be able  to link the member more directly with the club, providing information of interest, digital  membership card, access to digital facilities, calendar, etc.      Selection    In this creation process, several Design Thinking tools such as Mind Mapping and  Rapid Prototyping were used along the way, so the team became interested in the  Learning Launch technique to test the designs based on all the previous information  collected.      Application    Two days were held at the club's facilities to test the ideas and prototypes. On the first  day, stakeholders such as members, parents, former members and the general public  not associated with the club were invited to test a first version of the mobile application  resulting from a rapid prototyping. These people were interviewed according to a  rehearsed script to make them feel as comfortable as possible, and were discreetly  filmed for further behavior analysis. From this day on, valuable metrics emerged for the  following stages.      Insight    From the commented sessions, it was possible to extract highly valuable data to  identify what the user's workflow was like within the application. Thus, certain screens  with options were observed that presented a higher level of difficulty than the one  desired for the public, which led to generating new designs and tests to reach a  satisfactory result. The web application was also tested with people with some degree  of visual or motor disability, to guarantee its accessibility and access without any type  of restrictions.      Approach    Given that our team has implemented other Design Thinking tools in the project in the  past, we believe that a Learning Launch is the appropriate tool to continue

incorporating improvements iteratively from the insights, and in this way to be able to  get closer to the final product that has meaning for the public as well as for the club.
target:

input:
Collecting Sponsorship by Using Learning Launch

Challenge and Selection

I, Md Shamim, am an executive of workshop and event management  department of Strategyzer AG which a non-profit organization. Our  department’s main work is to arrange seminar on skill development and host a  grand carnival every year. But arranging carnival is not easy task. Every year we had  to do a lot of work and plan to get sponsorship as it is our main source money.

So, we hit a plan to set up an experimental workshop for upcoming carnival and  that is why we took learning launch tool to use to design.

Application

First, we launch an online quiz contest to attract audience that we need most to  make a successful carnival. Basically, we targeted students as we work for students’  skill development. So, we advertise this online event on social media like facebook  and got a very high appointment. Our main goal was to attract investors for  acknowledging our social activities and that was going as our assumption.

But we did not stop there. For our second learning launch, we had some fund left  after arranging previous carnival and seminars and with that we conduct a  workshop on IELTS Higher Study; 2 months before carnival. In this workshop, we  got a worthy number of students and we invite our pre-targeted investor to come  on this workshop to give space to talk about corporate world and its responsibilities  as well.

After the workshop almost every investor which we have targeted was very  interested to give us sponsorship within 3 weeks.

Insight and Approach

Implementing learning launch tool, we were able to identify our investors and our  demands as well and design launch helped us to raise our fund quickly. Our team  already have gone through to learning experiment we are still trying to make  another new design model which will be more precise but our team needs to  review previous learning launch experiment well.

input:
Kamrul Javed 14/11/2018

Visualisation tool for drone development

Challenge

I own a drone solutions company and my/our challenge is to always improve our products and  services. Currently we are in the process of developing a new drone. With this we need to come  up with something new, something diﬀerent. Find our target audience and work around their  needs to create the ultimate drone.

Selection

As we are designing the drone in house it is clear to start with the visualisation tool to discover our  needs, our clients needs, designs, and put our ideas into a picture. Allowing us to put our ideas  together and think about possibilities we haven’t seen before.

Application

For our ﬁst visualisation tool we started with everyone in the team putting up their ideas on a  board in picture, word and design form. This lead to lots of discussions about diﬀerent types of  idea and brought up new ideas which we also added to the board. For the second part we took all  our ideas and started to see patterns, situations it can be used for and diﬀerent segments. Our  challenge was to pick out which to leave out and focus on whats important and doing so in a  group, everyone is diﬀerent and cant agree on everything.

Insight

By applying the visualisation tool, my team and I identiﬁed our key solutions and design ideas and  built a better understanding of where and how we move forward and create a more eﬃcient  workﬂow. The results were much better than expected and we though more broadly than we have  in previous meetings, discovering several new use cases for our drone which we will be pursuing.

Approach

This new way of discovering ideas had a big impact on the team and is deﬁnitely something we  are going to continue using in our brainstorming phase. Moving forward we will analyse all the  information we have put together and use it to generate story around it in video form and put the  customer experience and usage in focus. Then push it out to a select group and analyse the  responses. This will give us a better understanding of what the end user thinks of the product and  if theres anything we’ve missed in our design thinking process.
target:

input:
Submitted by Tracy Simmons

DESIGN THINKING: PEER REVIEW ASSIGNMENT / TOOL: MINDMAPPING

CHALLENGE:

One of the key consumer mega  trends is the emergence of  ‘Experience Economy’ where  consumers values Experiences  more than Materialistic  possessions.

While this was a huge  opportunity, in India, it was  observed that this trend was still  in its very nascent stage with  more than 80% of the  population staying at home or  travelling within a radius of 5km  from their home during  weekends. Moreover the needs  and the barriers were very  diverse for different people

The challenge therefore was to  decode the latent needs and  develop a platform which  enables them to go out, explore  and discover new experiences

Since the challenge was very  broad and abstract and would  have different solutions for  different personas, Mind  Mapping was used as the  primary tool for this exercise

Therefore, Mind Mapping was  used to synthesize the various  interview data, identify the  different personas and think of  possibilities to solve the  challenge

Video Learning Reference: Week  3  - The Idea Generation Process  and the MeYouHealth Story.

Step 1: Capturing data through Empathographies (made-up  word to imply ethnographies focusing on Consumer  Empathy)

Step 2: Once there was enough data, each consumer was  bucketed into a 2x2 and then each grid was detailed in terms  of needs, motivations, aspirations to find patterns and form  relevant personas

SELECTION:  APPLICATION:

LUXURY

SOCIAL GRATIFICATION

PERSONAL  GRATIFICATION

THRILL

Submitted by Tracy Simmons

DESIGN THINKING: PEER REVIEW ASSIGNMENT / TOOL: MINDMAPPING

Identified the following for each persona: − Context of the Persona − What does experience mean to the persona − Needs, Motivations & Barriers

… and  more

Employed Projection techniques to understand  the aspirations of different people:

Which is the Super hero that you would want  to become and Why?

This helped in understanding of What Wows for  these customers and gave the underlying theme  for the ideas. The Core insight which emerged is  that Experiences which were tuned to their  Challenge Quotient inspired them to travel more

This changed our thinking from “Looking for  that one set of experiences” which would  motivate people to “Personalizing an  experience” for everyone to motive them

INSIGHT:  APPROACH:

An alternate approach to solving  this challenge could have been  “Storytelling”

The key step there would have  been to develop an engaging story  for each of the persona and then  explaining their journeys.

This would eventually lead us into  understanding the journey for each  persona (“When I take my car out  on a weekend for a long drive, I  will…”)

input:
Example reﬂection - storytelling by Sara Perez

Challenge & Selection

I use the design tool storytelling because I feel it can connect business with people. Not only

is it useful for making new strategies and brand positioning, but also to create trust and

loyalty with our customers. In my process, ﬁrst, I deﬁne the business story in three parts:

introduction, confrontation, and resolution. Second, investigate who the audience is, where

they come from and what they need, so I can conceptualize brand values and improve its

communication channels. If I don’t have tools like that, I only create empty stories without

purpose.

Application

I use storytelling to create content for a brand on Instagram. First, I have to do research and

meet the client because I need to ask a lot about new ideas and know what needs to be done

to conceptualize its brand values. I seek to develop content that highlights human factors

and the brand's tone of voice. It has to be sincere and short copy as my aim is to connect

emotionally with customers and not bore them into the process. It has to contain the

inspirational idea that there is a person with a difﬁculty and tell how they overcame it. Once

the writing is ﬁnished, I read it out loud to hear how it sounds; I also ask my friends how it

sounds and what resonates with them. After making the necessary corrections, I publish it

and measure how the audience responds to see the impact it created.

Insight & Approach

Texts created from storytelling improve the communication of brands because they make a

more natural and empathetic relationship with the audience. Society is looking for this type

of connection in a globalised world full of companies looking to sell their services or

products. The important thing is to stand out from the rest and create a brand tone of voice

that is related to the brand values. Therefore, storytelling is an interesting way to tell a story

and connect with the needs of your customers. I would like to keep improving my writing

skills through practice to create more emotional stories because it is important to tell good

stories that change the day or the perspective of the people who read you.

input:
Innovation on Cafe

In our local area, I found a café which was recently modified. Before that, the  business was not that high, but the business has increased rapidly. The  modification means the Structure of the café, Tables, and the atmosphere of the  café by setting new things. With the increase in the techniques and the varieties  of coffee around the world, it is time to evolve to other processes and Methods.  The café has introduced new offers for the customers and taking the feedback to  increase the availability of the products and new concepts. The chance of  innovating new techniques is multiplying, and we, the users, can modify the  needs how we want and make use of new things. To create new things, we have  to think in a new way and allow ourselves to think differently. We can choose the  varieties that people like and should know what things people are interested in  and modify what we don’t know. So we should select accurate tools and select  new businesses. Designers come up with their thoughts and ideas and help to  generate new ideas and grow the business. A growing company has many tasks as  compared to existing companies. They should increase the market of the business  and expand the business. They have also given reasonable offers for the  customers and made things happen. We should improve the tools and give more  time to increase the business.

By Sharan Chauhan

Design thinking for innovation

Assignment submitted by: Abdul Nasar

1. Visualization (Module 1)

Challenge:

As an aircraft quality advisor, I need to interpret the design requirements needed  by the client and I must communicate it to our engineering team in such a manner  that everyone understands it clearly. This clear understanding helps the company  to see the customer’s vision and make it true if the idea is viable. To remove the  gap our company always arranges a one on one conference, where both the client  and the design engineers will discuss the possibilities, options etc. For a building  that engineering marvel. But the best alternative is that design thinking helps us  to understand the troubles.

Selection:

Visualization is the best instrument to understand the problems and limitations.  Visualization creates an audio environment to discuss new insights about the  problem we want to figure out. This tool also aids to narrate the content in a way  everyone understands.

Application:

After utilizing these techniques, applying this is the most important component. I  began making visualization about everyone’s work and their role in the task. I set  about describing their roles in the chart and showed this to the chief engineer, he  was surprised to see this. The chart is still in the position. Visualization helps us to  proceed to the next step because we agreed on what the problem is and drive the  efforts to work on the best results.

Insights:

Not everyone in my team owns the same English understanding capability. This  was the serious issue in our company. But after the inculcation of the visualization  technique allows us to outline and write what we think and it likewise constitutes  the operation easy. Because everyone understands the matter, the time frame to  complete the project scale drastically. Thanks to this technique.

Approach:

Visualization technique’s approach is rather gentle and elementary. But a pair of  markers, and a chart (just a typical thing in an office right). While explaining  combining with hand gestures and powerful speech the company believes in me  that I will successfully finish the project. After utilization the technique our  environment became vibrant and rich.

input:
Mind mapping a design tool – Abdul Khan

Challenge & Selection:

Mind mapping is an important tool that can be used in design thinking process.  Especially when a new  initiative or design decision to be made.   We use mind mapping to choose a Pharmaceutical product to  be launched and when to launch and where to launch.  It is important to make apt decision on what,  when and how while launching the product.  Success factor of the product launch depends on the how  well organized and affective the product launch is being done.  Market acceptance and effectiveness of  the product.   Normally the products are launched to the market somewhere between one to two years  from the decisions are made.   Since our products are pharmaceutical products, product registration  process will take few months before we get an approval to market.   As such we also need to see the  feasibility of the product foreseeing a year ahead.

Application & Approach:

Initially a team consists of key members of Production, Marketing, Sales, Logistics & Product Manager  will have a brainstorming sessions on various potential products using mind mapping tool.  Prior to the  mind mapping session, the Product manager will give an overview of the selected products and then we  will have separate meetings on a different date, this gives time for the participants to think on the  products that were projected.   During the brainstorming session meetings, we make separate sheets for  each product to draw the mind mapping for each product name and then branch out all possible  scenarios including a SWOT (Strength Weakness Opportunities and thread) analysis.  Initially we won’t  focus much on the financial and ROI part.  However, after streamlined the best two options will be run  through the financials for a better decision make process.

After the financial process, a final proposal with business plan will be submitted to the CEO for final  approval.   This process normally takes between 1 to 3 months depends on the complexity of the  product.  Since our products are pharmaceutical nature the process need to be one of the fine-tuned  and meticulously planned.

Insights:

We found that mind mapping is very efficient tool and this will help us to systematically do the design  thinking.  It is easy and narrative to see the feasibility of a project proposal through mind mapping.  Though the sessions used to have heated discussion on product identification and selection.  Once it is  being selected and accepted, it is the team responsibility to work together towards the success of the  product launching.  Combined team responsibility makes the success.

input:
Final Assignment

Leonardo Giles

Challenge & Selection

The challenge I chose is to help young people (college and university), who have  difficulty in socializing and have mental health problems; since they do not have  confidence in openly asking for help from their parents, teachers, classmates,  among others. The tool used for this challenge will be the mental mapping tool,  which I found very useful for interacting with this group of people, and getting the  report that they can provide for possible solutions.

Application

The first action that I will take will be to contact several people, who have gone  through the problem in question, have coped with it in a positive way or are still  facing the problem, and are willing to share their experience that will serve as a  reference for young people who are in the similar situation.

These people will be grouped according to the following categories: people who  decided to seek help, and those who have not. Based on the aforementioned  categories, they will be presented on a blackboard with an idea for their category.  The former will contribute ideas based on their experience and what led them to  request help, as well as what kind of help they would have liked to have; while,  for the second group, they will contribute ideas of why they have not wanted to  seek help, there have been reasons of disgust for asking for help, among others.  At the end of the activity I will gather and organize all the ideas contributed to  determine the best factor to help and solve the problem.

Insight & Aproach

What I have learned from carrying out this activity with the selected tool is that  the more people are involved with facing the challenge, the greater perspectives  will be obtained to solve the problems of the vast majority of the people who face  them and not just a minority. similar to my thought process. In addition, we could  involve people who do not have this condition but who have the empathy to help  their peers.

I think that for the next occasion I would support myself with the visualization tool,  since it has a similar objective to that of mind mapping, but with this tool it will be  possible to reach a global vision of the problem and, consequently, its solution.

input:
Date: 22/09/2020

Name: Farjana Iqbal

Challenge and selection

Storytelling is a spectacular tool to reach out to people's minds and feelings. It's a great technique

that I use with our clients. I work with individuals who have Alzheimer's disease, who need support

and intense care. I spend much time inspiring and focusing on their feelings rather than using

words. I struggled a lot to understand their desires and emotions, so I devoted a specific day in the

week to communicate with each other and to share each story they have, even if it's from their

imagination. They always have a different story to tell. I use the information I gained from these

sessions to ascertain their goals and interests and figure out different plans and strategies to know

what they lack and need to overcome

Application

The real story behind using storytelling started when I was in a campaign to support orphans. I

tried to explain the importance of hearing people express their feelings and addressing their

individual needs. I found out that the organizing company failed to contribute to the children and

did not know what each child desired. Although it seems hard to ask each child what they want,

it's an excellent opportunity to create a better environment for them. I started posing a situation

that confronted me when I was at school. I remember an orphan girl who had always been bullied

and scorned in class. She used to set outside class to cry every day. I could tell she needed

immediate help. When I encouraged her to challenge her fears and not be ashamed to say to the

teacher. She started realizing my words and noticing it might make a difference. All she needed

was support and love! I started telling her my story when I was bullied in the school bus, I was

beaten and insulted every time, but I confronted my fears and told other people who helped me

interfere with this problem

Insight and approach

Well, surprisingly, she started feeling comfortable and realized that she has to do it! She started

opening up and told the teacher, and finally, she stopped the bullying. I learned from what I

experienced is that you could inspire people by telling your story. Storytelling is a potent tool to

consider. You can reach an individual's feelings and emotions. You can change something

eventually! I told this story to my colleagues in the campaign here when they started changing

their ways and policies to contribute to the orphans in a completely different way.

This course taught me how powerful storytelling is. I can make a significant difference in the future

with my ideas using the various techniques I learned. Now I'll manage my problems and challenges

in a very different mindset.

input:
REFLECTION

VISUALIZATION

By Melissa Martinez

Challenge

I am a university student in the last semesters of Administration. Last semester I took a course on  Development of Creative Potential. Throughout this class we addressed different creativity  techniques and for the final evaluation we were asked to do a work as a team in which it was  necessary to design a creativity development program as if we were going to implement it in a real  company.

My team and I develop the points of the work and prepare it for a first review. Our teacher put  particular emphasis on that our projects looked 100% school-based and that we should make them  look professional, like a formal company would present a program in real life. My colleagues seemed  quite disoriented, they didn't know how to make our work look professional. I offered to do it and  tried to explain the idea to them.

Selection

I am not a professional nor do I have much experience in the business world of work. However, I am  a mostly visual person and I like to work with these elements.

As mentioned in the video conference that talks about this tool, visualization is quite important in  our way of communicating. By having to use the right side of our brain (often ignored) we can think  non-verbally and be more creative. That is why I chose this tool, because the impact that an element  has on our view is what will influence it to attract our attention or go unnoticed.

Application

The first thing I did was a quick evaluation of the job we had, it was pretty ugly and it didn't attract  attention at all. Right away, I myself visualized in my mind how I would like it to look and feel  professional, creative, and impactful.

Then I looked for graphic elements of all kinds to highlight my document and applied them:  headings, images, graphics and figures, color combination, letters, emphasis and distribution of the  text, etc. The result was amazing even for me, it was truly professional and eye-catching work.

Insight

I discovered the enormous power of visualization, even when I already knew its importance. He  encouraged me to submit professional and carefully done work beyond written information. It also  took me out of my creative limits and out of my common thinking. Now I think that it is an important  element in design thinking and that I can always improve.

Approach

I would like to keep improving this tool for my own use and to help others as well. I would implement  other tools to expand and improve my ideas and my teamwork, creating innovative or different  things than usual. I would go through the entire design thinking process or use tools that I acquired  in my class.

Challenge and Selection

A key area of my job scope is to develop step-by-step digitalization guides for the  small and medium enterprises (SMEs) of various industries and sectors. The SMEs  are important to Singapore, collectively employing 70% of Singapore’s workforce and  account for almost half of Singapore’s GDP. Hence, digitalisation of SMEs can have  a profound impact on Singapore’s economy.

However, SMEs generally lack the technical know-how and find it difficult to divert  resources to digital transformation. Hence, it is important to develop a guide to make  going digital simple for the SMEs.

To develop a guide that is most suitable to the needs of the SMEs, I plan to conduct  a series of workshops with various SMEs, and use the Mind Mapping tool to  generate insights, collect information and create design criteria.

Application

In the first workshop that I conduct, I will gather information from the SMEs to  understand their experiences in digitalization. I will get each SME to fill up a form to  provide me with information regarding their digital transformation journey, such as:

-  Current mode business operations (manual vs digital)

-  Areas of improvement for business operations

-  Challenges that SMEs when digitalizing

-  Obstacles that are stopping them from digitalizing

In the second workshop, with the information consolidated, I will set up a room with  walls filled with the common problems faced by the SMEs in the industry and the  SMEs can go around the room to understand the problems and think about how  digitalization can come in to help solve the problems.

In the third workshop, SMEs will be put together in groups to share their experiences  and problems, and most importantly, how digitalization can help. The SMEs will then  work together, to discuss on how they want to digitally transform their businesses in  step-by-step manner.

Insight and Approach

We have vast amount of information and data regarding the SMEs, and we thought  we knew what SMEs need. So, we developed the guides for the SMEs without  gathering and consolidating in a structured manner. However, we noticed that SMEs  did not find the guides useful.

With the mind mapping tool, I believe that we can better organize the information  provided by the SMEs, draw insights from their businesses and scope the design

criteria to help generate better suited ideas to be included in the guides. Bringing the  SMEs together and getting them to map their needs and requirements together, will  provide SMEs with best-suited guidance in their digital journey.

It is important to view from the consumer perspective. In my case, SMEs are my  consumer. To provide them with a product, the step-by-step guide, that can benefit  them and help them digitalize, it is vital to hear from them on what they need and  want.

Written by: Md Hamid

input:
Guddu Singh Reflection of Design Tool Application

Challenge and Selection:

I was working on a project and needed to communicate the magnitude of the discrepancy  between men and women in consulting. I needed to show how gender representation numbers  change over time as you progress into the higher levels of consulting. This was going to do into a  powerpoint deck that later made the case for offering unique networking opportunities for  women. I knew that, in order for the Ask later to be strongest, the audience really needed to feel  the disparity that exists at the highest levels of consulting.

I was deciding between using visualization and story-telling as a means of really connecting with  the audience and making this part of the presentation impactful. Ultimately, I decided to focus on  the Visualization tool. I chose this for a couple reasons: firstly, I thought of the saying “a picture  speaks a thousand words.” I knew that if people saw not just the numbers but the numbers  reflected in visual form, that would be more impactful. Secondly, I this tools is one I am less  comfortable with, so this also felt like a prime opportunity or practice.

Application and Approach

I ended up taking the data in Microsoft excel and spent time thinking about different ways to  visualize the data. I ended up deciding to start with showing a pie that starts out showing 50%  men and 50% women at entry level roles. I then removed respective parts of the pie as we moved  up the ranks to finally leave just 17% of the Partners were women. At the same time, the portion  of the pie that represented men keeps growing at each level up the ranks. So we start out with  parity and end up showing how the disparity looks by the end.

Insights and Future Application/Approach

I learned that the more I am able to communicate with visuals, the better the message lands with  an audience. This might sound like a really obvious point to many, but my default has always  been to use words to convey points. This exercise pushed me to invest my time in trying  something different and it was really powerful. I also realized, while I was playing around with  different visualizations, that it matters a lot HOW I choose to visualize something. I think the  most effective visualization will also be the most elegant in that the “takeaway” is clear. There  should not be confusion about what an audience member should take away.

I think I want to keep practicing and I want to collaborate with a partner in the future so that  there are different perspectives in the design phase. It also helps to think of the end goal, the  thing I want to communicate.

input:
Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge & Selection

Storytelling is today the main tools one haves to promote your brand, product or idea. For the last  10 years, I’ve been involved in educational projects, specially focused on millennials. In order to  understand how to better communicate the information we wanted our audience to learn we asked  ourselves two questions: what millennials are interested in, and how they consume information. The  conclusions showed us that they are more willing to consume information when is given by someone  in the format of a story, for example, we saw a huge increase in Vlogers that talked about academic  issues like geography, politics and history, and they were very successful in their channels.

Application

We decided to tell our story as our main educational tool. Instead of talking about general history,  we started the conversation y telling our personal history. Before even talking about complex  realities, we talked about our personal challenges and dilemmas. Our classes became sessions and  the structure of teacher and students disappear. We were equals talking about real issues, learning  about how history could help us overcome our challenges by learning from it. Another very  important aspect is that we encouraged the participants to have a conversation with us, as  millennials are so used to do in social media, we gave them an opportunity to tell their stories.

Insight & Approach

Education is a challenging process, and with every generation we should study a better way to do it.  Sadly, formal education is less open to changes. Our results show that, when applying storytelling  techniques to education, engagement grows and students are more open to learn.

By telling our story we became humans to the participants, and they were interested in hearing  what we had to say. More interestingly, we were able to discover that they had a lot to say too and  they realized that they knew more than what they thought about the subjects we were talking about

Now, I work in an executive manager position. Until this course I was not able to see the use of this  technique to my new job. This course allowed me to better understand how I can use techniques  from different backgrounds to create more holistic plans for the nonprofit I manage.

I think design thinking, in combination with classic techniques of project management, will allow us  to create programs that adapt better to the new reality of the world.

Mauro Pacheco

input:
Eman Rahaman Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge & Selection     Coming from an English Literature background putting things into a story seems second nature  to me. Storytelling is one of those things about humans that makes us drawn to one another and  feeds into our curiosity.   I work in a media agency with numerous diverse clients that all belong in different industries,  and i’m challenged constantly when tapping into the client’s mentality and making sure that our  creative content stays relevant to the brand’s guidelines and still maintains a creative standard.     Application    Working in an agency that has client fluidity has multiple creative challenges, you are faced with  numerous creative blocks having to steer from one client to another. However, applying the  storytelling tool clears away a lot of confusion in the sense that it narrows your creative thinking  into a smaller scale, not by limiting the possibilities, but by focusing on what makes every client  unique by centering on its key points.   For example, one of our clients is a tongue scraping product that wanted a campaign to raise  more awareness into the products’ sales. First thing I suggested was to know more about the  product itself and all of it’s characteristics, what makes it stand out? Why isn't it selling? Who is  it targeted to? Basically create an entire character list customized for that product.   The more we knew about the product and who they wanted to target the easier it was for us to  cultivate a campaign suited for it. For example, the product was best known to get rid of  unpleasant mouth odor, and in a Middle Eastern country that celebrates Ramadan and fasts  one month every year bad mouth odor is quite common and troublesome, thus we personalized  the campaign to run during the fast while marketing its usage during fasting, so that not only will  people want to buy the product but also it has a sense of personalization     Insight & Approach     By applying the storytelling tool we were able to crack into the product’s core by truly  understanding what it is, who its for, when its best suited for use, and a lot of other details.  The results were almost effective immediately, people started responding on the products’  social media page about how the product helped them on a personal level in the sense where  they can stick to where they stand religiously without having to suffer hygienically, especially  when we knew even more about why people get bad breaths when they fast and normalized it  to be an act of normality rather than of abnormality, thus prepping our product as a norm rather  than a luxury.

input: Challenge & Selection     “You have always been good in storytelling” my friends and family said to me in last ten years. I  can tell about my dream like a novel or a movie. They like listening to me. I must confest that I  like telling. Even my anecdotes amused them. I know it is about how to tell more than what you  tell : )  I have lots of stories about this but I will tell you the last one.       Application   My friend, Mohammed, wanted to learn German language but he always feared to give up early. He had not no  time to go to a course. I recommend him a software “Rosetta Stone”. He couldn’t believe because a  software cannot achieve this. I needed a story to manipulate him. I knew the programme and I really  knew how beneficial and functional it is. I said “Mohammed, this is amazing pogramme. You have ability to learn a  language. I had a friend who wants to Yiddish language to live in Israel. And I think it is more difficult  than German language I think. He started to learn from Rosetta Stone and got some books, and got  some friends on web. He couldn’t believe how he proceed. And do you know what? He lives in Tel Aviv  now. But in real I had no friend like him. Nobody wanted to learn Yiddish and lived in Israel : )      Insight & Approach     I was sure my insight was true. He just need a encouragement. Mohammed doesn’t still know the truth about  Yiddish. But Mohammed can speak German in intermediate level. Sometimes we just need a mini spark to flare.  And a good storytelling can do it easily.

I knew the programme and I really  knew how beneficial and functional it is. I said “⟨NAME⟩Mohammed⟨/NAME⟩, this is amazing pogramme. You have ability to learn a  language. I had a friend who wants to Yiddish language to live in Israel. And I think it is more difficult  than German language I think. He started to learn from Rosetta Stone and got some books, and got  some friends on web. He couldn’t believe how he proceed. And do you know what? He lives in Tel Aviv  now. But in real I had no friend like him. Nobody wanted to learn Yiddish and lived in Israel : )      Insight & Approach     I was sure my insight was true. He just need a encouragement. ⟨NAME⟩Mohammed⟨/NAME⟩ doesn’t still know the truth about  Yiddish. But ⟨NAME⟩Mohammed⟨/NAME⟩ can speak German in intermediate level. Sometimes we just need a mini spark to flare.  And a good storytelling can do it easily.

input:
Kashi Papa

Example Reflection – Storytelling tool.

Challenge and Selection

I am an SME Account Manager for a big telecommunication company. My organization every year  (before the pandemic) organize a weekend for all B2B sales employees including back-office. We are  gathering in a hotel for an “all-together weekend”. During this weekend a lot of activities take place.  One of the activities, which includes bonding activities like games, is brainstorming in order to make  create ideas for the organizations for future launches.

My team decided a few years ago during one of these weekends, to ask from the Organizations to  create a software tool which every Account Manager would have at his tablet and when he visits any  client would have in real time all the information that he needs about the client phone lines. He could  also use this application to create new contract for new phone lines of existing customers.

The tool we used to make our proposition is Storytelling. We need as much as possible information  about this software tool.

Application

During this weekend time we discussed with all the account managers about their needs explaining  to them that addressing their needs could have a huge impact for all of us. We discussed about every  day activities and how this tool would improve the meetings with the clients. We also contacted with  some clients and asked them about their opinion, what kind of information they want from their  account manager to have with him during their meeting. We also discussed with senior managers  about the privacy issues and what information would the organizations allowed us to have in real  time during client meetings. Everyone helped us and our proposition prepared.

Insight

During the us of the Storytelling tool we identified our assumptions. Other Account managers did  things with a different way as we did, they also spend different amount of time with every client. We  also find out that some clients needs more information and more quality meetings with them. We  also find out that the time we (the account manager) would spend with every customer would highly  increase and so less clients meetings would take place every day. The more important issue that  appeared is the privacy issues that appeared from the organization. Moving forward our team review  the key issues and in the final propositions we included all the information that we find out.

Approach

A lot of information appeared using storytelling tool and our proposal was a really good one as the  organization also admitted. After software would be launched by the IT department we could also  use the Learning launch tool in order to improve the software and to add more information on it. I  think that this course gave me an excellent opportunity to learn improved techniques and think things  with a different way.

input:
Mauro Zizza, DESIGN THINKING ASSIGNMENT    DT for psychotherapy: The role of story telling    I am an engineer and I became an specialist in Machine Design and Design Methods. For  reasons out of the scope of this essay, I became a psychotherapist in several approaches,  one of them being existential therapy.     In some point last year, I realized that I operated sometimes in therapy under the scope of  design stages and thought of having a method based on that. This is not the method itself  but a reflection on the coincidences and how a DT is affected by the method itself and, if  seen correctly, it is also a healing process for some.    What is?    When I have a new client or a regular one, I alway ask: what do you want to work with? So  they begin telling me a story. At the first stages I use the phenomenological method to keep  asking what is? Just the data, no judgements -for now. Sometimes this is enough since the  person can change his/her story by clearing the facts and is able to separate his/her own  judgements or prejudices.     What if?    In a second stage we begin to explore what others would think. So first I ask: What do you  think I think about it? Some times they are very close and others not. So I continue to ask:  What if you see things like I do? Then we can ask: what do he/she/they think about our  session? What if you ask them? What if you propose/suggest/explore what you found out  today about yourself?    What wows?    A regular question to clients is: what are your options, your wishes, alternatives? Very often  a client is locked out and see no options. Many of the idea development from this course can  be used as a game  during the session. I use many materials. Again, one option is to have  the client re write the story with two, three, four different endings.    What works?    Therapy sessions is a safe place to try out experiments. And there are hundreds of them to  inspire the client to look out and try new behaviours. Usually they “feel” what will work for  them even is not the most safe choice out there. The experiment in the wider world can fail,  but that in itself is a success since the person is trying out new versions of himself/herself.  Returning back to therapy with a “failure” is a chance to iterate in the design process and  pick up one of the ideas the client discarded or graduate the experiment and try it out again.     Story telling

The whole process is about telling a story, rewriting it, adding chapters, considering life as a  novel where a new chapter can be written, or revisiting a previous chapter and rewriting it. In  the DT course, I liked when they talked about having empathy with those involved in the  process. Well I practice the so called humanistic approach to therapy and being empathic  and teaching that to the client is central.

input:
Reflection – Visualization

Talaat Mohmmed

Challenge and Selection

As noted during the course, “visualization uses imagery to make abstract ideas more concrete and  public.” During the innovation process, it’s easy when explaining an idea like a crowdsourcing platform  to colleagues for it to take shape in various divergent ways in their heads. Though divergence is often  helpful during the ideation phase of the innovation process, it sometimes is necessary to be able to be  able to achieve a mental match, and the visualization is most effective in doing so. An idea can only  move beyond the conceptualization stage, only when consensus is reached. I tend to use visualization  during every stage of the design thinking process. As you move throughout the process, the visuals  mature and a concept become less abstract, kind of like an image becoming less pixelated.

Application

During a recent design sprint, throughout the problem-framing and ideation phases, my colleagues and I  thought we had narrowed down who was the end user was for a crowdsourcing application for the  humanitarian sector. We were being tasked to solve why local and indigenous knowledge of eco-system  based early warning indicators of floods are not integrated into disaster risk reduction programming,  despite the awareness that they work. Several problem trees led us to the root cause of the problem,  which was that humanitarian aid workers lacked the documentation and validation of insights from local  knowledge holders on utilizing nature-based indicators for flood prediction.

Insight and Approach

Only by drawing out a diagram connecting the information providers (indigenous and local communities  of natural hazard prone areas) to disaster risk reduction practitioners (humanitarian aid workers), we  realized that a major and obvious component of our solution was missing and that we indeed had two  end users. We were focusing all our energies on developing the data collection tool but neglected to

think the method through which crowdsourced information would be housed and could easily accessed  by humanitarian workers to “co-design” their programs.

This course reinforced my belief that visualization is one of the most helpful and crucial tools to use for  design thinking.

I have a background in international development and humanitarianism, and always firmly believed that  programs should be designed with and for affected populations. Design thinking provides the structure,  tools, and frameworks to be able to do just that.

input:
Example Reflection – Learning Launch

The term design thinking was something I was constantly running into and although I made a mental note to learn more about it I put it in the back of my mind and kept forgetting about it. After finishing this course I am sorry it took me so long to get around to it. To say I enjoyed the course immensely is an understatement.

When faced with the final assignment my mind went blank until the moment I realized the serendipity of the position I found myself in.

Challange

As a designer and a comic strip creator, I was asked to ideate a comic strip character, for a series of comics that will be featured in a trade magazine with an educational purpose. The comic strip character should help raise awareness with higher management staff not willing to adjust to modern technology and business methods. In short the “Old school CEOs”.

The problem is how to ideate a character that will be likable but not offensive, but still impressive enough to attract attention and a following.

Selection After following the design thinking process and researching higher management personas, my proposal is that the best course of action is to do a learning launch. The purpose of a learning launch is to test the product or service before launching the finished product to the market allowing us to test the validity of the idea and the reaction of the market. As explained in the video Learning launch Tool in week 4 of the course.

In the case of the creation of a comic strip character, although not a typical product, all rules, and best practices apply.

The main purpose of this learning launch is to observe the reactions of the intended public and to see if the main purpose is achieved, that is that the character is not considered offensive and the educational lessons delivered through humor are well received.

Application

The process of this particular learning launch is quite simple, the chosen audience is given a few versions of the finished product to read after which they will answer a short survey and answer a few open-ended questions by the interviewer to collect impressions and comments. Assuming the audience receives one or all products, the comic strip can be prepared for publishing.

Design Thinking for Innovation, Iva Juric

Insight

If the acceptance is lacking then with the insights gained from the learning launch the ideation of the comic strip characters will start from the beginning and the process of the learning launch repeated

Approach

The process will start with ideating 3 sample characters with their own comic each consisting of a series of 7 comics to be shown to an audience consisting of at least 3 “ Old school CEO” personas with a total pool of 10-15 people. The reason why a few versions of the product are presented at the same learning launch is to come as close to a “winner” product as soon and as cheaply as possible.

Design Thinking for Innovation, Iva Juric
target:

input:
Using sTOYrytelling by Roberto Pink

http://www.frederick-curtis.net/posts/wp-content/categoryauthor.php

Challenge & Selection    The purpose of this challenge was to discuss how the communication between users and  developers influence the outcome of a process of developing a software. Specifically  contextualized from the agile development perspective the proposal was to discuss how  narrations of history could enhance and improve elicitation process requirements. So the idea  was to adopt the storytelling paradigm directing the focus of studies for application in  software engineering processes. The storytelling is closely linked to the development of  mankind, and use techniques and strategies they use narratives to capture the audience's  attention and lead to certain actions of subtle and natural way has become quite effective.

Application

These photos are bottom of the page, we were discussing how to innovate in products created  in technology companies, using design thinking. Was applied storytelling using toys,  participants rode on the scene and with it we can see that the focus should always be on  customers / users. Before the process within the company for software creation was  bureaucratic, we thought only within the time limit and agreed financial values. But now  processes have changed and customer satisfaction comes first.

Insight & Approach

It is often necessary to teach your client some things, from instructions on how to use products  or services, to strategies and best practices in the use of tools. Normally, manuals and step by  step guides are boring and too long to fulfill the role of teaching what is needed.

Use characters (who are also representing the consumers themselves) within a story is the  best way to teach something more absorbing content, illustrating situations and desired  solutions.

Every client has a history and it can be good enough to promote your product or service.  revolutionary ideas usually solve old problems.

The conclusion that I always have to use Storytelling is that it is a very powerful tool! It can  help in the building of a brand, helping its growth and boosting its image with positive  associations to the public.

Storytelling has the power to encourage the emergence of "brand advocates" creating strong  emotional ties with your audience and conveying your message effectively and objectively.  Whether for training, consumer education, to inspire or discuss the news and market trends,  the storytelling is an excellent choice.

That's me, coordinating

the sTOYrytelling.

The conclusion that I always have to use Storytelling is that it is a very powerful tool! It can  help in the building of a brand, helping its growth and boosting its image with positive  associations to the public.

Storytelling has the power to encourage the emergence of "brand advocates" creating strong  emotional ties with your audience and conveying your message effectively and objectively.  Whether for training, consumer education, to inspire or discuss the news and market trends,  the storytelling is an excellent choice.

That's me, coordinating

the sTOYrytelling.

input: Example Reflection – Learning Launch   Challenge   I am Luca Ciccone, Egyptian, Teaching Assistant at Teaching Assistant at  Macmillan and Innovation Ambassador at  Ho branch according to  Communication Ministry. Our problem in Egypt is the big gap between  scientific research and practical application in the labor market. Our team  is working to try to find a solution to this serious problem.     Selection   Our team has already gone through several design thinking activities/tools,  so we are ready to try the learning launch tool. Broadly speaking, the  learning launch is an experiment meant to quickly test an idea and  prototype.     Application   For our first learning launch, we focused on trying to find a way to link the  university parties and companies by obtaining a website to communicate.  For our second learning launch, we focused on the meeting between the  two parties where contact was made with the MLAND and the  J Gs Cupcake store company in Benha, as well as the Zurak Cancer  Foundation, and  thus our hypotheses were validated.    Insight   By applying the learning launch tool, our team identified our assumptions  and designed launches to test these assumptions. By practical experience,  we found the companies ’need for university research to develop their  products, but the company cannot fully finance the research or purchase  the basic equipment needed for development. Through this result, the  project was modified through the introduction of an intermediary  company to finance equipment from Saudi Arabia and the United Arab  Emirates. The ongoing incorporation of insight is particularly important at  this stage, as launches come before a full rollout in the design thinking  timeline.    Approach   Our team has already gone through several design thinking activities/tools,  the learning launch is the appropriate tool.

input:
Andres Castro

Visualization tool

Challenge

As a member of one service organization my challenge is to find new ways to make better the  process of the service (i´m talking about to answer calls and make a report of it) i have to increase  the speed of the service in order to give the best treat. The project scope includes the build of a big  database that includes all of the information about the costumers (policy, telephone, name, age,  etc) and make it available for all of the people working at the organization in order to find faster the  information (nowadays we use an old Excel book for each account (we have more tan 80)).

Selection

The visualization tool is a very nice component of the way we use to communicate an information  acurate. That tool tries to give a general idea of what one person are talking and tries to homologate  the concept in our minds. It it a powerfull tool to make easy the prosses of an idea generation.

Application

The first thing I gonna do is make an animation of the process that i developed to show my partners  how this new idea will change and make better the work times making easy to achieve our goals  and make better our quality feedback. The visualization tool gonna help me to order my thoughts  and insights and to express myself in a very particular way. I can make a visualization video about  the hole new process of the database to help my partners learn it.

Insight

By applying the learning launch tool, our team identified our assumptions and designed launches  to test these assumptions. Additionally, we thought through anticipated results, expecting to find  that a more agile approach would improveour workflow.

Andres Castro

Approach

Future launch visualization tools should reflect insight gained, progress made, and come closer to  a compelling solution ready for full rollout.

input:
Peer-graded Assignment: Reflection

For this activity choose Storytelling (Module 2) Mind maps (module 3) in order to successfully

carry out a very important networking project in the construction of the Accenture headquarters

building in Buenos Aires, the challenge was to change the mentality of the team of technicians

who were unmotivated and did not work as a solid team I was very thoughtful because the

project had to be delivered on time but the obstacle was the technical team, there were no

other barriers, neither economic nor infrastructure, materials and equipment, we had

everything, but the lack of motivation and lack of companionship was overwhelming putting the

project at risk.

My idea was to hold daily meetings of 15 minutes applying agile methodology with the

technicians and we chatted about the performance of the work, but also about soccer and

tactical stories and thoughts of famous coaches, for example Pep Guardiola among others, this

decompresses the work environment and everyone They are passionate about soccer, these

tactical stories and epic matches motivated them and changed their mood, the performance

was increasing and thus they themselves were able to create their mental maps and contribute

ideas to the meetings.

In summary applying Storytelling (Module 2)

Mind maps (module 3) I was able to achieve a collective mentality change in the team of

technicians, solve a problem creatively in the Design Thinking style and as a result close the

project in a timely manner.

Jimena

30-12-20

input:
Design Thinking for Innovation - Mind Mapping  Juan Flores

Challenge

I’m a software engineer student at Sathyabama University and my principal goal is to improve reading interest  specially on teens, in my opinion this would cause positive changes in reading comprehension and  linguistic enrichment, adding an improvement in spelling and speaking. I am going to focus on thinking  strategies to attract people's attention especially to young people. I’ve chosen this challenge because is  important to understand and analyze what are you reading to.

Selection

I’ve chosen mind mapping because I can add ideas as many as I want without respecting a concrete order  and applicating my own best distribution maybe using techniques as brain storming, nevertheless I can  compare and analyze the ideas I’ve thought and then select the best of all.

Application

I’m going to use a kind of marketing focusing on something that attract the people which we want to  convey this message or this proposal for me I would use various resources such as talks which motivate  students reading to make such a reading a habit would then use various types of methods such as in- person lectures which give reasons to read, lectures which explain more the importance and need of  reading, these lectures would be more intuitive, more participatory, etc. One aspect on the internet we can  make videoconferences to give reasons and make dynamics to encourage reading in young people,  another would be that we can give access to online books which would be more attractive for young  people since they are used on their mobile device, another of the online forms would be through  audiobooks since some young people do not like to see letters very much because the r They produce and  listen to them anyway, we would give some attractive books such as science fiction, action, among others,  since they are the ones that are read the most and the last of my ideas would be like audio books.

Insight

By chosen mind mapping y was able to add ideas as many as I want without respecting a concrete order  and applicating my own best distribution using brain storming, I was developing, such as the lectures, I put  them in the face-to-face space and online, I also analyzed some trends as an explanation and joined them  with ideas that help attract the reader.

Approach

The use of mind map helped me a lot because I could organize my ideas and use the best of all in order to  show a proper article.

input:
Diego Estrada

Design Thinking Assignment

Visualization Tool

Challenge & Selection

The elderly were having a hard time adapting to the changes we brought in our bank. As  a result of a poorly implemented linear solution, a more customer centric approach was  needed.

After learning about design thinking in this course, we decided to apply it to solve this  problem. The visualization tool allowed the team to create a dynamic presentation using  diagrams, figures and drawings on the go that really resonated among the stakeholders.  Previous to this change, none of our solutions seemed to be adequate for them, but the  new implementation created a different type of connection with them that helped them  understand the problem in the way the team and I did.

Application

The process starts in the prep time. The team uses a series of tools and software to  develop a presentation using the surveys gathered during research and the solutions we  created during the process. The use of graphs to quickly show statistics in a fully visual  way, rather than verbally was a game changer.

After having a presentation prepared, the team hands an activity to the stakeholders,  where the solutions discussed previously appear. Nonetheless, the solutions need more  work to them. After this. The stakeholders are asked to help complete the solutions  while the team and I create diagrams on a blackboard to represent how their  suggestions would impact on this specific problem.

The use of a group activity strengthens the bond between the company and their  investors. It makes them feel like they take part and help solve the problems as well as  show how customer centric the solutions are. Every complaint and suggestion from  customers are read and evaluated using the graph shown in the course (Involving: can  we do it? Can we afford it? …). The finalization of this activity leaves the team and the  stakeholders on the same page. It allows them to completely understand and feel part  of the solution and also gives them the chance to ask better questions, which eases the  work of the team.

Insight & Approach

The use of this method created a new workflow in the Design Team. It increased the  productivity and the success rate as well as the customer/stakeholders satisfaction. The  use of the visualization tool created an engaged group of people who work together to

Diego Estrada

find a solution based on their customer satisfaction. This solution is later revised and  tweaked with the help of the stakeholders who are deeply involved in the process.

Presentations, graphics, and activities have added a huge increase in satisfaction. As a  company we also learnt that engaging different areas can be difficult because of the  varying levels of understanding, but when paired with the adequate process things just  flow.

(This story is fictional and was created for solving the assignment)

input:
ENSURING UNIFORM FRUIT QUALITY

By: Lalisa Cruz

Challenge

In my last employment, I worked in a start-up food manufacturer in Metro Manila.  I was the sole R&D  personnel in that company, and part of my job was sourcing suppliers of fresh fruits.  Having no prior  experience with procuring fruits, it was a huge challenge for me to find a good, reputable supplier to  transact business with.  As soon as I was able to find a few good ones, the bigger challenge for me was to  ensure that the quality of the fruits is uniform among the suppliers I found.

Selection

I selected the storytelling tool for this particular challenge because I believe that it is the best tool in  obtaining the relevant information I seek.  Since the challenge was for me to ensure that the quality of  fruits is uniform among the suppliers, I had to know the postharvest processes that each of the suppliers  employ.

Application

I talked to the suppliers individually.  In a casual manner, I was able to obtain non-proprietary information  from them through the storytelling tool.  They shared with me their postharvest processes and from there,  I found what specific processes produced the best quality fruits.  As such, I was able to determine the  particular supplier to use as benchmark.  With the supplier’s express consent, our company adopted these  practices as subsequent process steps for those lower quality fruits coming from other suppliers in order  that all the fruits have uniform quality.

Insight

This tool effectively improved the uniformity of the quality of fruits.  From the experience of using this  tool, I was able to gain the following insights: (1) storytelling is as effective as objective approaches; (2)  storytelling gives us relevant baseline information which we can work our improvements from; and (3)  design thinking, in general, utilizes the lean six sigma approach.

Approach

I wouldn’t use any other tool in this scenario because the suppliers’ postharvest processes are internal to  them, and so it is only through storytelling that they are able to share such information with people  outside their organization.

input:
Simon Nkabinde: Company General Use

Storytelling

Challenge and Selection:

The first tool that I used was storytelling in order to bring language learning alive and create  participatory and immersive experience that allows the listeners to enjoy and entertain in a way.

Diagnostic imaging procedures are cutting-edge technology, but at the same time they are an  unpleasant experience for patients especially children. I remember when I saw a little girl who was  continuously crying on her way to the scanner. I suddenly noticed that this room that contains the  scanner looks just like a brick with a hole in it and with kind of dark and those flickering fluorescents and  I realized how scary this experience was to all patients. Therefore, I spent a time with those patients and  have focused on children and used stories to visualize and understand their concerns. I used this  information to develop different strategies to try and solve this issue by trying to find a way that works  for them and how to create scanners that patients would love.

Application:

I started the process by telling a story with a company that has interests in new technology and  discussed with them the need and importance of these diagnostic scanners in general and what benefits  they can provide. Then, I began to explain the urgency to figure out and study how changes can be  implemented to these scanners that will make patient fall in love with them. I started by sharing a story  about the little girl that was on her knees crying and followed it with the story of an old man who could  not enter these scanners and after several week when they finally used anesthesia, they found out that  he suffers from brain cancer. These scary incidents should be taken to consideration otherwise we will  lose more patients in the near future.

Insight and Approach:

Well, wow, that was an eye-opener. The agency offered tools that ignited my confidence and I learned  about human-centered approach to design an innovation. Going through human-centered design  process with people in diverse industries and roles from management to human resources to finance. I  started to imagine how powerful this tool could be if I brought it back and got cross-functional teams to  work together. I observed and gained sympathy for young children at a day care center.

I talked to child life specialists to fully understand what pediatric patients went through and reached out  for help from people around me, including a small volunteer group from a local children’s day care  centers and doctors and staff from a nearby hospital. After that, I created the first prototype of what  would become the adventure series scanner.
target:

input:
ADNOC Classification: Internal

Design Thinking for Innovation 01 Nov 2015.

Peer-graded Assignment: Reflection

Submit by Dec 25, 11:59 PM PST

Submitted for peer reviews by Dla Xalid   Email: vmartinez@hotmail.com

Refilling fuel at ADNOC Petrol Station at Abu Dhabi City.

Challenge to avoid the Bottle neck for Rush hour, busy service providers at Pump filling  point, Full Car Parking, Crowded at Payment point.

Storytelling Tool is selected to resolve the issue of time delay and cars crowed during rush  hours at Petrol Station.

1. Collected Data for time duration and Customers service staff engagement at Petrol

Station.

2. Collected number of cars awaiting during rush hour.

Using the tool provided options to resolve the issues:

1. Once car enters petrol station to select appropriate filling pump lane.  2. Avoid rush hour and plan alternative time for refilling fuel.  3. Pay for fuel by bank Credit card.

The benefit of applying the above options while communicating with customers and Petrol  station service providers:

1. Positive Feedback from Customer stories, the Satisfaction and Happiness.  2. Able to serve customer with an efficient.  3. Enhanced timing requirement to refill fuel.  4. Win to win situation

I might use similar tool for challenging cooking and delivering Pizza to customers on time  delay after placing the order and serve hot Pizza.

input:
AGILE: Company General Use

Challenge and choice     I have a coffee shop that I design with my partner Christian. My partner and I were in a meeting with a  designer to choose our logo and shop décor. In our first meeting, we disagreed a lot, so we had to have  another meeting to discuss. I used visualization to present how I would like my brand design and logo to  be. I brought in experienced people to explain our business concepts.     Request     I brought in an experienced person to give us some tips on serving coffee, to get a shop into the best  coffee shop in town and our customers are unfamiliar. Accordingly, our motto should be unique "Be  different". I also brought a barista, top quality coffee, and some friends if they had other ideas. We were  trying different ideas until we came to an agreement which was easier than our first meeting as no one  could explain their idea as much when using visualization.     Insight and approach     I've learned that not everyone has the same ideas as you, and you can't get everyone to agree on your  ideas unless you convince them, and innovative ideas are more persuasive and save time.

input:
MY SINGAPORE EXPERIENCE

Challenge: To build a world class team of ~15 electronic engineers from the scratch for an MNC  company at Singapore and execute a challenging multi-site project in 15 months. This include their  hiring, ramp-up and deliver a project.

Selection: Storytelling

After the successful assignment in Sanjose, USA and when we (My family) are about to fly back to our  native country India for good, I got a call from one of my ex-colleague, friend from previous company.  That call was about the above challenging opportunity mentioned as part of “Challenge” section.

So this is about the experience that I went through both personally and professionally at Singapore. It’s  evident that the tool “Storytelling” is an apt one 😊

The video lecture by Lucio Bush, Mars Inc., exactly fits in my above journey as first I need to form a  team of human beings with right attitude, experience, diverse backgrounds and CAUSE right from hiring  phase.

Application: Each of the team member had been handpicked carefully with a right experience and  attitude to put them into the right slot.

Once onboarded I spent lot of productive time in bringing everyone towards the common cause by  explaining them what exact problem that we were trying to handle. It’s also about listening to their  perspective along the journey.

It’s just not about list of project tasks in hitting the timeline of 15 months. It’s about logic, emotional  connection and larger purpose in crossing the line with top quality customer project.

Insight: The team of 15 members is very diverse with a mix of people from Singapore, China, Malaysia,  India and Indonesia. Truly the Asia Pacific 😊

It’s always good to have this team mix of diversity in handling this innovative, technically challenging  project in wireless domain with identified small steps of Fast & Cheap experiments (Learning Launch)  within the team.

Apart from technicality, Imagine how do we typically celebrate the success moments?

The fun used to start with simple question like which restaurant we choose (Indian, Chinese Malaysian  or Continental !!)? Of course we used to huddle together in a room for about 5-10 mins to arrive at a  consensus on this 😊

This project was a huge success in the end and first time silicon success and lot of mass production and  so the profits to the company. This is an example of when we give freedom to the team with a serious  common cause (right Storytelling), wonders do happen naturally!!

MY SINGAPORE EXPERIENCE

Approach: Storytelling coupled with Visualization would have been more effective in this mission of  forming a team & successful execution. Visualization ahead in the game with respect to work  assignments and proper work load would have been an even nicer experience without stress in the  system.

Best Regards,

Keith Khan

Once onboarded I spent lot of productive time in bringing everyone towards the common cause by  explaining them what exact problem that we were trying to handle. It’s also about listening to their  perspective along the journey.

It’s just not about list of project tasks in hitting the timeline of 15 months. It’s about logic, emotional  connection and larger purpose in crossing the line with top quality customer project.

Insight: The team of 15 members is very diverse with a mix of people from Singapore, China, Malaysia,  India and Indonesia. Truly the Asia Pacific 😊

It’s always good to have this team mix of diversity in handling this innovative, technically challenging  project in wireless domain with identified small steps of Fast & Cheap experiments (Learning Launch)  within the team.

Apart from technicality, Imagine how do we typically celebrate the success moments?

The fun used to start with simple question like which restaurant we choose (Indian, Chinese Malaysian  or Continental !!)? Of course we used to huddle together in a room for about 5-10 mins to arrive at a  consensus on this 😊

This project was a huge success in the end and first time silicon success and lot of mass production and  so the profits to the company. This is an example of when we give freedom to the team with a serious  common cause (right Storytelling), wonders do happen naturally!!

MY SINGAPORE EXPERIENCE

Approach: Storytelling coupled with Visualization would have been more effective in this mission of  forming a team & successful execution. Visualization ahead in the game with respect to work  assignments and proper work load would have been an even nicer experience without stress in the  system.

Best Regards,

⟨NAME⟩Keith Khan⟨/NAME⟩

input:
Reflection from Enrico Laterza – Visualization tool

Challenge & Selection

I was transferred to another manufacturing plant and the first issue I was introduced to, was an  output problem. We had a production line, which was able to produce two kinds of products.  Those two products had a demand higher than we could supply. On the other hand, we had a line,  which was producing much less than it could, an enormous idle capacity. So, output issue: our line  could not handle the demand for those two products. Another line with idle capacity. That is what  we had. Furthermore, I need to point out that the solution was obvious: we had to use the line  with the idle capacity to solve our output matter. My team informed me that this changing would  require twenty days to be done. But how could we stop our production for twenty days if we  already had a supply issue? Impossible. The time was our biggest restriction.

With all of this pointed out, I decided to use the visualization tool. It helped a lot, my team, to see  the problem and the steps needed to solve it. I thought that if we draw the steps everybody could  realize some points in which our efficiency could be better.

Application

I started drawing a flowchart with our biggest restriction on the top. Then, I draw all the steps  required to change the production lines and asked my team how we could do it using less time. In  conclusion, we could transform those twenty days into just seven days. Thanks to the visualization  tool.

Insight & Approach

I learned that if we use the correct tools, we could break every paradigm. We cannot accept some  restrictions, which are imposed on us. We must stand up, think, choose the best tool and apply it  correctly. Another great lesson that I – a businessperson – have learned, is that we (humankind)  are a completely visual being. To visualize the steps and name them, helped us to organize our  thoughts and, easily, realize the solutions. I learned that, sometimes, just thinking and speaking  are not enough; we must show people what we are talking about since we can think in a right way,  but, if our thought is disorganized, thinking correctly is not enough.  Design Thinking taught me  that.

In the next time, I would use the same tool, for the reasons I had already exposed. We are visual  beings. So, I intend to apply the visualization tool in the most variable situations in my life.  Although, I would do something differently. Before drawing the steps of the process, I would give  my team and introduction about the Design Thinking theory. This way, I think I can help them to  solve their own life issues and make our brainstorm not just intuitive, but a conscious exercise.
target:
