input:
Luis Hernandez

STORYTELLING

Challenge & Selection

Storytelling is an excellent tool that design thinking gives us. It helps us a lot  because the process of empathizing is the most important thing in design thinking  and through a story we can make our audience feel included, understood and even  make them smile. I select this tool because personally it is my favorite since I love  the way the stories are told, I find it fun for the public and also because it is  something so well elaborated you can reach many places with it. The fact of  studying, analyzing and developing a story for the promotion of our product is  something fun and often moving. I share stories that have marked both me and my  family in order to generate a special bond with our customers, and I also use this  link to improve because thanks to your comments I can improve either my product  or the way I share it with the public.

Application

To carry out the storytelling I can think of a story in which people feel included,  for example, a household product. The story begins by revealing a person who has  a boring routine and who does not enjoy being at home, nothing interesting  happens in his life but one day he finds a bird that follows him to his home, he is  bored lying down when Suddenly he realizes that the bird poops on his table on the  terrace, he is forced to clean but then he realizes that he had not been on his terrace  for months, without enjoying it and with that the idea comes to him of having a  party in it, and when you have everything ready, you realize that you need a grill  and that is where the solution occurs: a semi-professional grill at a good price and  easy to order. It is a story that occurs to me a bit quickly, I could refine some  details, but this is the way that storytelling is carried out, with this I can go to a  company that is in charge of selling this type of product and tell them how we can  promote their products.

Insight & Approach

Undoubtedly, storytelling has been a tool that gives us a lot, with the story told  above it can be deduced that such basic products can complement something as

Luis Hernandez

important as socializing, getting out of the routine a bit and that is how we also  empathize in people. Thanks to this course I can see in a different way everything I  need to be able to make my ideas clear and thus have more possibilities in the  world, have more options and be able to see beyond what I have currently done.

input:
Reflection Tiago Borges about Mind Mapping

Challenge    The challenge chosen was around and Onboarding process in an IT Company. The idea  was apply the design thinking tools to understand how the process happened and  improve the processes using the tools. Mind mapping is a great tool inside the Design  thinking and was used to understand the gaps that the process had and how was the  things that could be improved.   The case is that when an It company hired new employees to work as analysts for  different customers, there was no an onboarding process. That process was done  thought the project culture, generating a lot of none equalizes in the answers to the  end users.    Selection    I chose the mind mapping because we haven’t sure about the process like happened  that moment and We would need to understand how was the things that could be  improved. Using the ideas learned at the course, I started the exercise with 10  employees, mapping the process like happened.   After this, we had more 2 phases, to get the pain points and the improvement ideas. In  the end of the exercise, we used the periodization matrix to choice the best  improvement ideas.    Application    The application happened using one session, with 10 employees of the company,  working as managers (different level). We had 3 hours to run the 3 phases of the  exercise, covering the process, pain point and improvement ideas.   In the end of the processes, we had 15 steps of the process like was running, 23 pain  points and 15 improvement ideas. We chose 11 ideas as measure ideas to apply in the  process. Analyzing this numbers, I believe that the process was a success, effective.     Insight      When the exercise started, I think that all participants don’t know about the  simulation, the exercise and about the ways that Design Thinking can improve the  process. I had to show how the things would work.   The presentation of Design Thinking changed the mind of the participants and I believe  that. As a result, we could experiment an hard difference in the process using the new  way, created using Design Thinking. Now, we have an IT onboarding process to the  new joiners.     Approach

I applied the tool selected, using prioritization matrix as an support tool, to help us to  decided how was the ideas that we would like to choose. We used effectively the Mind  Mapping.

input: Carlos Pereira: Reflection on Mind Mapping    Challenge:  My problem was related to the high incidence of clinical depression in a nursing  home. This situation came to me through a request for help from one of the  health professionals, a nurse, from the institution; referring to the high number  of polymedicated patients, both for chronic conditions, with cardiovascular,  metabolic and respiratory disease, as well as medication aimed at mental health  – causing high financial and personal costs for the elderly.    Selection:  My first thought was to apply "What is?" to clarify the situation, trying to find the  real problem, or cause, for depression in this group of elderly people.  In this way, I carried out a set of interviews (ethnographic research) in an  attempt to understand what these people felt, what their days were like, what  problems they perceived, what they liked to see improved in their lives.  Given the above, I chose to use the Mind Mapping technique, in order to  gather all relevant feelings and perceptions, so I can gain insight knowledge  from what was really keeping those people from being happy and have better  quality of life.    Application:  In order to carry out the proposal to use the Mind Mapping technique, several  visits were made during 1 month, in order to be able to speak with all the  residents of the home, as well as with the providers.  During these visits, the problems of both parties were heard, which was felt and  perceived by patients as problematic and subject to improvement (subjective);  as well as evaluation feedback from home providers (objective).  We were able to notice that the absence of playful moments as well as limited  family visits in the post-COVID-19 era; accentuated by dissatisfaction with the  food in the canteen, were a problem for these elderly people.    Insight:  By applying that technique I learned that not only the present times (COVID-19)  were limiting these persons happiness, but also there was a hidden  insatisfaction with the food services.   These results are in part expectable, because I knew that it was likely to be  related with social distancing consequences. But the application of the Mind  Map made a hidden part of the process visible.    Approach:  Given that this technique solved our problem by giving different insights  exposing problems that had not yet been identified, I think it was the right  choice.  Neverthless, I assume that it could have also been useful the use of a diferente  technique such as Storytelling or Journey Mapping which, unconsciously,  were equally applied - which leads us to conceive Design Thinking as a  holistic and integrated process and not as separate parts.

input:
Giuseppe Alberti – Design thinking for innovation

Challenge

I’m a professional photographer, based in Brazil, and weddings are my

main niche. Through this course I’ve learned some tools and methods, and now

I’m able to put in practice some ideas that lived in my mind for the last year.

As I talk to my clients, I hear some frequent problems that brides go

through, when planning their weddings – things that go from finding the right

professionals to the way that a ceremony must happen.

Selection/Application/Insight/Approach

Based on that, my idea was to develop a course, so that the brides can be

more prepared to the problems that appear during a wedding plan.

And here enters the design thinking: I’ve interviewed some brides, being

or not my clients, to have a deeper understand of the issues that they go and

gone by.

Focusing on the problems of them, I’ve learned about the core pain they

face, and confirmed my assumption, that the course will solve some serious

problems. I will now proceed and test ways to do. If it becomes necessary,

pivoting and looking for alternative paths to solve the problems I hear about so

often.

I’m already using the knowledge acquired in the course classes.

input:
Classification : Giuseppe Petrosillo - Confidential El Petrosillo 7/1/2019 12:02 AM

Visualization Tool

Challenge and Selection

I am working as a tool manufacturing engineering team leader in a well-known factory which is in  automotive sector. Moreover, my team is responsible from whole tool process that are using at assembly  and machining lines. The tool which I have used is visualization. The probability of work accident is very  common in a factory environment. Sometimes, it depends on an operation tool in a process with non- standard activities. Recently, an accident was happened related with tool. I evaluated this incident with  my team via visualization tool.

Application

Firstly, I showed technical drawing of the tool for being sure that we all are in the same page. Furthermore,  we have watched video of the related process for getting ready to brainstorming. It is a way to get people  concentrated.

Secondly, I have drawn a fishbone diagram for finding possible root causes. I wrote problem and main  related topics with different colors and shapes. We discussed all main topics one by one. Everyone focused  on the same thing and understood each other clearly.

Lastly, after finding root cause we started to work on actions. For example, we sketched the design of a  new tool on the board.

Insight and Approach

I have learned from using this tool is that, the main point is understand the situation without any comment.  Visualization is not only the way of being parallel inference but also the way of triggering creative solutions.  Moreover, valuable aspect of the activity is seeming a positive union team with no doubt relevant subject  or creative solutions. Solutions were coming in sight very short period because of clear problem definition.

For future subjects and processes, I am going to use visualization tool whole steps for having rich  understanding, getting solutions and efficient teamworks with another point of views.

input: Hi my name is Silvia, I am a student at  the IIT. First, I would like  to talk about a little bit of mind mapping.  Mind mapping is one of the most powerful tools  in the design thinking toolbox. it's used to look for patterns, it's used to look for insights,  that lead to more creative solutions to your design challenge.      Mind mapping is a tool that I use most of the time in my school, and it has helped me to  make my projects look positive in a creative way. Right now, I am a student at the  IIT (located in Mexico), and I carry out subjects in which they make  us interact with other people doing teamwork. Practically most of the time I spend  interacting with people, talking about problems, and we always try to solve them by giving  each our point of view, and finally arrive with the best solution and the best idea.  However, this whole process has not been easy, and thanks to the Mind Mapping tool it  has helped us to organize our ideas and to better understand the situation.    I have a story specifically in which we use these kinds of tools, this was in a subject called  "reflection project," in which we had to gather several people to think about a problem  that people with disabilities suffer, and makes it difficult for them to enter a public place  specifically in a hotel.     We got some ideas on how to solve this problem, however our ideas were many and not  very specific, but thanks to the Mind Mapping tool we were able to organize more. Since  each of us had different ideas, we had to expose them in an innovative way ,which by this  means (using Mind Mapping) the other colleagues were able to understand with what we  wanted to understand , that is, the possible solutions to the problem . Finally,this is a type  of tools more visual than conceptual , therefore also the project we chose could be better  understood.    The ideas we put forward were different as well as making a mechanism of escalators so  that people with this disability could move from one place to another easily ,We also had  the idea of making an emergency bracelet with which I had the purpose that with a sensor  , they inside the hotel could contact those who work in the hotel and so be able to locate  them by means of the device ,and thus attend to them in what they need. Finally thanks  to this tool we were also able to use it for the results we obtained when making our  prototype.    It also helped us a lot to organize our results that we got from doing the hotel survey, and  so they told us their opinion about this project. Finally, I can say that even people outside  our environment made us some comments, which many of them concluded that thanks to  our organization , innovation, and creativity (by using a Mind Map), they were able to  better understand the project, and the results were successful.

input:
Reflection

Name: Akhilesh Kumar

Challenge & Selection

To start this writing, I need to talk about design thinking as an enablement and a very  important quality since it allows different ideas to be developed globally

As an experience I would like to count the time I decided to sell fast food as a way to  generate extra money during quarantine

Application

First of all, I had to think that I wanted to specialize the food service, I had to debate  between burgers and fries or sandwiches and fries.

Analysing with my brother (an economist) he told me that there were many emerging  burgers stands in the sector which would be a difficult competition but very profitable  lunch since there were no burgers at a good price and with respect to sandwiches, we  analyzed the competition and realized that there are few sandwich places. It could  clearly be said that the option of sandwiches was chosen but it is not so, since there are  few restaurants of this food one can understand that they have rotation.

After having chosen the burgers, I started trying different flavors and types of pairing,  also taking into account the final price of the product. Since the idea was to get a  product that is tasty, unusual and with a fair price for customers. With this in mind, I  bought the necessary ingredients to do a tasting with my family and select the burgers  that met the requirements we were looking for.

We started selling the product, focused on the people of the north of the city and people  we knew (family/friends), our cheapest product was a Burger with fries for eleven  thousand pesos and the most expensive was a mixture of quite a few preparations that  made an exquisite flavor together with beef achieving the pairing sought above. I was  the only one who works in the kitchen since starting to sell was up to me, but that didn’t  take away the fact that my mother helped me cut certain things while I prepared other  products.

Insight & Approach

It all ended when I began to feel quite tired since the job was demanding and the kitchen  felt small for so much workload

input:
Mario Pedrini’s Reflection

Design Thinking for Innovation - Storytelling

Challenge & Selection    Once upon a time I got a major in engineering, thereupon I was “formatted” to see and  work with numbers, applying math, physics and some other exact sciences, which should  not represent any inconvenience to overcome my day-to-day on my own.  When I started my company, I could be a particularly good engineer dealing with most of  the problems given to me by my clients, and I could test my knowledge as well as the ways  to solve the issue.    As an entrepreneur I had to overcome diverse angles in management, those that I had just  glimpsed during my days as student, and one of the must -terrifying- situations was selling  my services to strangers, specially over the phone performing a cold call, no matter having  a script in front of me.  Generally speaking, when trying to convince potential customers to purchase my services,  the conversation was too technical, and the context lacked feelings.  Often both parties  were heading different destinations, therefore I had to use an extra effort to persuade and  get my goal. When the job had been finished the appreciation was good in terms of time  and quality of delivery.    Application    Hence our proposals fulfilled the requirements, the relation client-contractor was not  totally satisfying because a touch of magic was missing.  Let’s not forget that B2B is done  between people.    At some point and since technology has evolved like greased lightning, -storytelling- arose  as a helping hand when dealing with emotionless operations.  As a touch of magic, the use  of real stories when talking about businesses made the clients turn and pay attention in full  when required during the process of sale.     Insight & Approach    When using storytelling, stress was cut of the situation and emotions were involved  immediately when talking about common past experiences.  It is a must to put some “heart”  to touchless situations and cold things to get going.  After some time of practice, the use of -storytelling- has been developed abroad our sales  department, making both the clients and the sales representatives better related persons,  when our business spins with a human touch.
target:

input:
Jose Garcia

Storytelling

Challenge & Selection

When I was in high school, one of my teacher told me that I was a great storyteller,

in that moment I did not understand what that means. Currently I am a student of

Administration in Balatas ES (Politecnico di Milano) and I realized

that my teacher was not wrong about me.

Nowadays I am in conflict with one of my college team. The main issue is that he is

zero empathic with people and situations, that is the cause of many problems

because the decisions that we have taken is becuse that kind of thinking, affecting

the others and seeing just for our benefit.

As a lider, I am trying to manage the team in a empathic direction, is not easy

because they really do not understand a diferent point of view of themself. That is

the reason why I am practicing the storytelling.

Application

I have been using storytelling more than before, when we are discussing about a

project, I always find a moment to use this tool. I start talking about a specific

situation of the project and then I connect that situacion with a personal one. I finish

the story making a personal reflection, in that way, they can understand very well

different point of views clearer than just go in a direction.

For example, the last session that we had was the last week, we were talking about

the diagnostic needs of training. It was a very hard topic because they propossed

some agressive techniques for the collaborators, when it was my turn to speak, I

commented my story with the training and how I felt about it, I recalled to them when

we were on a similar situation and how a lot of them felt so dissgusted, in that way,

we are working to do making better deccisions focus in a humanistic approach.

Jose Garcia

Insight & Approach.

The team now are thinking about how they felt when my teacher applied to us a lot

of questionnaires, interviews, etcetera. We were really tired and at the end of the

day, the results were not useful. Detect needs for training is a hard work, specially

when you are not part of the enviroment that you want to train. We are not thinking

about questionnaires or explicit interviews, we are relly want to be part of it, known

te process, the fomal and informal groups, how to do it and see how they are doing.

I really consider that this course going to helps me to do it definitly better. Now I am

aware about a lot of tools, attitudes, etcetera. I often think about myself as a

entrepenaur, Mexico's economy is about that, and now I realized that my creativity

is a key to do it.

input:
Design thinking for innovation                          Coursera assignment                                 MIND MAPPING                                                                             Angelik

Mind mapping is used to represent how ideas or other items are linked to a central  idea and to each other. Mind maps are used to generate, visualize, structure, and  classify ideas to look for patterns and insights that provide key design criteria.

Challenge :

I was one of the process group  analysts in the team of a total 6 people in the project. The  project was part of a software development and artificial intelligence  programme of a  public authority. input:
Example Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge & Selection

Storytelling is an important design thinking tool. I use it to work with youth in difficulty in a suburben  area of Tunis. Those teenagers have communication difficulties, are aggressive, some of them live in  very difficult situations in families with alcohoolic or unemployed fathers that are violent with them  by assault or rape. Some of them quit their schools and become drug dealers or thieves. These  teenagers need to be empowered by culture, education and art . They need to be more confident in  their future and welth. I spend a lot of time finding ways to improve their circumstances and help  them become good and happy citizens. I work with them helped by some students from different  profiles : art students, ingeneers… We share with those youth stories about our lives to give them  more confidence and help them change ideas about life. We use stories to better understand their  fears, needs and interests.  Those stories help me convince local responsables in the community to  give those youth some facilities to do activities helping them well being.

Application

I used story telling with local authorities to get help and more involvment in the protection of these  youth in difficulty. Telling a story of a 13 years old boy Ashley who quitted his family house and lives in  the street. Ashley was used to be beated by his father every day. He decided not to return to his home  and to live in the street . He became a thief. I worked with Ashley and helped him express his fears and  calm his agressivity with art. Ashley changed his vision to the society and became more creative and  confident. He had the courage to face his family problem and returned back his home. Ashley is helping  me supervise other teenagers in difficulty and reduce agressions and violence in the community.

I also used story telling with those youth in difficulty to approach them and let them be more  confident to tell their own stories and describe their problems and fears.

Insight & Approach

Ashley’s story convinced the local authorities to invest in more cultural spaces and activities to supervise  youth in difficulty in the community. They became more aware of the importance of supervising  those teenagers.

Design thinking helps me have more structured ideas and take the best decisions for my actions. To  help teenagers in difficulty, design thinking is also a good tool for them, to make them increase their  leardership and empower their creativity.

Thanks to this course, I know more about the importance of storytelling when presenting my ideas. I  will use more this tool because it enables and allows easy contact and a better transmission of my  ideas.

input:
Frankie Kwan – Coursera – UVA Darden

Executive Education - Learning Launches

Challenge  I am working on a project creating executive education programs for middle to  senior managers, in collaboration with business schools. We have 3 team members  and have business development budget and 6 months of time on the campaigns. We  have already several hypothesis but we need to learn about our customers and  figure our their essential needs.    Selection  We implemented methods of learning launch tool, since we don’t have sufficient  data and we are looking for innovative solutions. We want to learn through quick  tests and affordable failures.    Application  We made a simple launching site and gauged 80 target customers for a quantitive  online survey, meanwhile through our personal network we got 10 participants for  qualitative interviews. Before launching the survey and interviews, we have  internally agree on the questions, content, and metrics, and we have also made trials  with a small group of people and made optimizations before launching.    Insights  After the survey and interviews, we found 8 pain points and 5 different type of  personas among our participants. We underestimated the diversity of our target  customer group and the priority of their key problems.    Approach  We decided to first focus a specific target group of potential customers, reframed our  hypothesis, prioritize key assumptions according to value creation upon specific needs,  our execution capabilities and competitive advantage. Then we designed the test for  those assumptions and conducted the tests.
target:

input:
Learning Launch Reflection

Rosa Perez

Challenge

Visvesvaraya

Technological University, family entrepreneurship brand focused on selling healthy desserts for  diabetics, vegans and healthy life searchers began operations at home. The challenge is being  known as an alternative way to enjoy delicious desserts sugar less and carbohydrates less but not  just for an event, we desire that a family could buy one cake every week.  The brand must be  known for a delicious taste and the best buy and delivery process, using technology and social  networks.

According to best innovation practices, we had some recipes using healthy ingredients and we had  some baking instruments but we didn´t know about the taste of the cake using stevia, panela and  oat. We need to know successful recipes, and listen people with experience in baking, selling and  delivering desserts. The best way to increase our knowledge was participating in baking classes  and participating in focus groups with experimented people.

Selection

We use learning launch like design thinking tool to prepare a cake prototype, we inspired on “truth  confirms assumptions about recipes, presentation and delivery process”. Every Sunday all team  members worked together in the process, we began with a chocolate cake recipe and at the end,  we tasted and expressed opinion about presentation, time spent and taste.

Application

First Learning launch, in the baking lab, the chocolate cake had good taste but the presentation  was terrible, our experience wasn´t enough to have a perfect product and we needed more  instruments like a mixer for example.

Second Learning launch, next week, the team agreed with taste and presentation but we needed  more products to offer, what happen with chocolate haters? Healthy pastry classes were perfect  to find successful recipes and a designer help us with the logo (brand identity).

Insight & Approach

We decided the timeframe (a week), cheap investment (ingredients and pastry classes) and it let  us knew that we must offer three products and detailed delivery conditions before the sale  agreement. The first market was our family members (with skeptics) and after we sale cakes on  demand. We offer different flavors and we have loyal clients.

Do I need to register  a beneficiary before  transferring funds  through UPI? What  details of beneficiary  will be required?

If I change my UPI  app will I be required  to register again or I  can carry the same  virtual address?

Is it necessary to add a beneficiary before  transferring funds? If  yes what details are  required?

Is the same virtual  address be used in  other UPI apps or  need to register  again?

No, registration of Beneficiary is not required for  transferring funds through UPI as the fund would be transferred on the basis of Virtual  ID/Account+IFSC/Aadhaar Number. (Please  check with your PSP and Issuing bank with regard to the services enabled on the App).

In case of change in UPI App, a person needs to  re-register and it needs to create a new VPA with the handle of the PSP.

In case my mobile is  used by another  person, will there be  any security breach?

Can I link more than  one bank account to  the same virtual  address?

How many bank  account we can add to same virtual address?

In any transaction, the UPI pin would be required which needs to be fed through the mobile at the  time of any transaction making it safe and  secured.

Yes, several bank accounts can be linked to the  same virtual address depending on the  functionalities being made available by the  respective PSPs.

Is bank account  compulsory or this can be linked to wallet?

Does this UPI transfers work only in the bank  hours or anytime?

No, customer cannot link a wallet to UPI, only  bank accounts can be added.

All payments are instant and 24/7, regardless of  your bank's working hours.

Questionnaire

(INPUT REQUIRED)  ❖ Electricity

➢ Does it work at home based output voltage?

■ Yes, it works on normal input current present at home.

➢ Would it also work on a dc input current?

■ No, as we have given the option of working on chargable batteries, dc

input current wont work.

❖ Battery

➢ Is it rechargeable or one time battery ?

■ We have given the option of rechargeable batteries.

(DRYING MODES)

❖ Hot

➢ Is the hot mode safe?

■ Yes, we have taken into consideration of the damage, thereby giving a

lower temperature .

➢ What is hot mode for?

■ It is for fast drying.

❖ Cold

➢ What is cold mode for?

■ It is to set your hair in the style you wanted it to be.

➢ Can it dry my hair?

■ The main purpose is sor styling and not for drying.

(PERFORMANCE)

❖ Fast dry

➢ What is exactly fast dry?

■ This feature is mainly for the people with long hair , for whom drying the

hair takes alot of time.

➢ How is that carried out?

■ There is a separate clip available to ensure more air outlet.

➢ Why is there a separate feature for styling?

■ For different kinds of hair we have given hair clips to style accordingly.

■ Yes, according to their needs we have.

➢ What is the price range?

■ Price varies from 3Thousand to 8Thousand rupees.

➢ Y the difference in the price ?

■ Different models have different output power levels, accordingly the

variations have been given.

If your child’s low on confidence in certain areas, instead of just asking

them to gain confidence, you can find stories of inspiring people to tell

your child in a way that your child believes they could be that person

and adapt their strategies and ideology.

She also talks about how storytelling is now being used in various

fields of business, in relationship building, etc, primarily because

storytelling releases the hormone oxytocin in us.

Oxytocin helps us feel happy and makes us feel connected to the

storyteller. This again, makes the bond between you and your child

stronger.

Our neurons fire information at 5 times the normal speed when we’re

listening to stories.

How to tell stories to your child:

• Vidya tells us how to respect storytelling as both an art and a

science. Make sure you’re emotionally expressive and excited  while you’re reading the story.

• Use actions and exhibit a positive and happy body language, too.

Read quotes by different characters in different voices and make  your kid laugh!

• Pick your moments to tell them these stories. It can be during

bedtime, while travelling, while spending time out with your  child, when they’re eating, etc.

• Read them stories in English, in their second language and even

in a third language sometimes. You can include words from

different languages when you’re reading stories from different  cultures to them!

Conclusion:

You’ll begin realizing that putting in this much energy into telling a

story will excite you and make you enjoy it.

A systematic approach for problem solving

I have used story telling tool to solve the problem .I preferred this tool  because it adds more effectiveness. First let me tell you the story about  a boy named Stephen, who is very poor and orphan. He used to work  in a tooth paste manufacturing company to feed himself. He is well  skilled and with in short time he was given a promotion as a supervisor  for physical department.

All was going well for few years. The M.D has decided to increase the  production. So, all of the board members have decided to attain a  meeting for the analysis of how to increase the company income. All  the team managers and supervisors had attended the meeting. The  objective of the meeting is “How to increase the company annual  income”. All the people have referred to the past census to figure why  the income has decreased.

Stephen has gone through his survey and his reports were telling  that the product has still maintained its demand in the market. Users  also like that taste. He noticed their product and its dimensions of the  mouth of the paste. He increased the gap of the tooth mouth by few  millimeters. All the team members were agreed to this plan and had  done a dramatic change in production. After that the sales have  increased for the product. Finally the company had succeeded in  increasing their production.

input:
My reflection – Storytelling

1. Challenge:

The first tool I used was the tool of Storytelling, to attract the attention of the participants,  and to introduce my topic in an interesting way, before stating the problem and the  solution. Storytelling is a kind of showing the problem from a real perspective.

2. Selection:

I select this approach usually to start talking in front of a group of participants in solution- driven projects and innovation labs, to present my idea as a creation from a real-life  situation. For example, I have used the story of a little girl which is my neighbor, who did  a great impact on the whole neighborhood to start recycling. This story improved my  concept that says: if we want to change the fruit, we have to change the root, what I mean  is: if we want a change, a new behavior in society, we have to start from the youngest  generation, which is our kids, and then, the solution will sure achieve the expected results.

3. Application:

I have started my presentation with this story:

Last Sunday, I was resting in my home, watching the news about the recent garbage  problem in our country, where I saw one of the most annoying photos that reflected a  really bad image of the behavior of the government and citizens at the same time. And I  was thinking about an effective solution as a social initiative to solve the problem.  Suddenly, someone knocked to my door, it was Federica, the 6 years old girl who’s living  in the neighborhood. She said: “Federica, from now on, you are required to keep your plastic  bottles in the vacant room at the ground floor. I will come back each week with my parents  to gather the plastic bottles to take it to the recycling center.”

What happened just made my thoughts valid and concrete. I was thinking that this is a  root problem that require a root change. Actually, the bigger generations aren’t ready to  start applying the recycling processes. In our educational system, the environmental  awareness isn’t available. This is where I decided that the solution should be starting  there, with the younger generations, the weapon of change.

Here is where my project idea came from….and I would like to tell you more about it….

4. Insight:

What I have learned from starting in a storytelling like the mentioned one, is really an  effective start and impactful from the very beginning. The attention lasted long to the final  word of my explanation. In addition, the emotional effect that a story could make is really  huge and can make all the participants agree and vote for the idea.

5. Approach:

Next time when using my technique of storytelling I will try to mention more details and  practice a more persuasive style. And I will try to give more examples from our real life.

input:
Rosa Jaque

The tool of Visualization

Challenge: I am an architecture student, since the first day of my career I have been facing different

challenges. However, I can say that creativeness and discipline have helped me to overcome them. For this

assignment I chose a challenge that consists on designing a rural community school located in Mexico.  The

project must be functional, sustainable and must minimize costs; and I will solve it by using “The tool of

Visualization” or “Visual Thinking” so have broad options.

Selection: “Visual thinking” is a way to solve a problem from the creative side of the brain. It is also a way of

communication. Visualizing is important in the designing process, that’s why I chose this tool because as in

architecture it lets you think in a none-verbal way.

Application: Visual thinking does not mean just drawing but to go further than words. For this project I

would try to first visualize spaces, materials and most importantly, how the experience in that place I´m

imagining will be. To make it effectively, I´ll create images such as renders so I can show to other people my

ideas and explain them easily. My intention is to sell ideas, to convince people to buy a project even when

they haven’t even touched it.

On the other hand, most people think that using cheap materials or cheaper construction methods is not

particularly good looking or secure, but I would like to demonstrate that it is not true and the best way is

through images.

Insight: In my opinion, to innovate results from exploring different solutions or alternatives for particular

situations and I think visual thinking is perfect for that. By exercising the creative side of our minds we could

redesign solutions when we need it.

Approach: I will not apply a different tool but I’ll rather complement the process previously described with  photography, so I can get more ideas that can help me build bridges to bigger projects than I could imagine.
target:
⟨NAME⟩Rosa Jaque⟨/NAME⟩

CHALLENGE AND SELECTION

The tool i used was the tool of mind map to create a new product for a cosmetics company and to sell that new producto to the custumers and position the company above the competition. The new product will have to be new among the others on the product ion  line, it is easily accessible, cheap and in demand.

The case will be the next one:

The Ayurvedic and Herbal Company wants to include a new product to its production line. The Company has in its line production the following  production lipsticks, blushes, mascara, eyeliners, foundation, translucent powder, concealers, shadows, and makeup materials. The  company wants a new product with more demant amoung the others. Beauty eirif is a micro company, with earnings below 3 million pesos a year, they have a branch in Mexico City and a website, they make  international shipments.

The tool that I will use will be Mind Maps because it is a tool that facilitates the understanding of the issues for this cas e and make a decision  based on the decisions, arguments, ideas and point of view of all the collaborators

APPLICATION To begin with the investigation, the BM team held a meeting and began with a brainstorming in phases;

New product in the production line •

Cheap production •

High demand product •

Profitable product •

Product that is of continuous use •

PHASE I: WHAT THE COMPANY WANTS

Cheap product •

Quality product •

Durable product •

Large product (in portions) •

PHASE II: WHAT THE CONSUMER WANTS

The team analyzed what the company can give to the consumer and the potential consumer was studied After having these data in a mental map, the requirements for the creation of the new product were better visualized and it was possible to see clearly  in which aspects they should be based on and not for decision making.

INSIGHT & APPROACH

Long duration •

Against the water •

Lasts 24 hours •

Certificate •

Guaranteed against irritation •

500ml •

Price $ 23 DOLLARS •

Works as a sunscreen, thus is the most important features to be able to say that it is a product that differs in the market •

After brainstorming and creating a mind map with all the aspects that were considered relevant for the decision making of the new product, it was  concluded that the best product they can add to the line and I approved all the requirements of the mind map is a MAKEUP FIXER with the next  features:

input:
Mutual understanding in AI implementation Visualization

Hiroshi Tamura

Challenges & Sections

The tool of choice for me is visualization. This is because I am an AI consultant, and whe n I implemented AI for a service, there was a perception difference between me and the c ustomer, and as a result, we couldn't have a meaningful discussion. We used a tool called  "visualization" to enable all stakeholders to share a common understanding so that they c an tackle issues from the same perspective.

application

I started by creating a simple table in PowerPoint with objectives and issues. However, th e conventional table that I compiled after listening to what I had to say was out of sync, s o I created a table for the AI consultant and a table for the client, respectively. In doing s o, we were able to see differences in their objectives and issues, and we were able to disc uss this part of the process and standardize the objectives and issues. I think this was effe ctively applied because the introduction of visualization allowed for a more constructive  discussion.

Secondly, you can easily share this material and review it immediately. This means that n o matter how complex an issue is, visualization tools can be used to simply and accuratel y share ideas, which can be quickly revisited many times. This allows for constant correct ion in a certain direction while respecting the ideas that each person has.

Thus, I utilized the visualization tool to have a more constructive and solid discussion wit h people who had different opinions.

Insights & Approaches

The insight/impact gained by this tool is that I was able to consider new ideas very simpl y but clearly. I'm not doing anything difficult. However, it has had a significant impact on  us for the better. We were able to sort out our complex feelings and visualize them so that we could understand each other's feelings, which led us to revisit our current issues. Reg arding the approach, this time we have applied the visualization tool to clarify the commo n understanding of the objectives and issues, but we also believe that it is possible to com plete a series of projects by visualizing the flow to achieve the objectives and visualizing t he current level of achievement.

Reflection: Storytelling

Challenge:

The challenge I had was to deliver a technical informative session to non-technical  colleagues. I’m an electrical engineer working in a massive oil and gas organization.  The department I worked on decided to have “knowledge sharing” sessions and I was  selected. I had to deliver a presentation about the country’s electrical grid network  to non-engineers which is quite the challenge since they do not have the basics in the  field.

Selection:

I selected Storytelling as I think it is one of the best available options to deliver the  subject presentation. The presentation will have to be smooth, non-complex,  interesting and easy to follow; thus, the selection.

Application:

First of all, while I was developing the presentation, my main goal was that the  audience will understand EVERYTHING. I didn’t want to explain a certain function or a  piece of information, that I might think it is easy and intuitive, but the audience  wouldn’t get it. So, I started developing the presentation slides showing visuals step- by-step telling a very “zoomed out” information then diving a little bit in and  explaining the details. Since I work in oil and gas company, instead of presenting and  telling that 5 MW is transferred here, I’d said a power equivalent to 200,000 barrels of  oil had been transferred in the form of power through these lines etc.

Insight:

For the above specific application, I really had learned that selecting complex  complicated words doesn’t make your presentation or work smarter, it is just complex  and complicated! When talking to an audience, talk in a storytelling approach which  would attract the audience attention which is ultimately the goal. The material was  presented to audience of approximately 100 colleagues, and I honestly got a very nice  feedback, people got interested and were asking questions. To be frank, storytelling  the electrical grid made it more fun and audience comprehended more information.

Approach:

The approach used is definitely adequate and helped me big time to overcome the  aforementioned challenge. If I had to select another approach for the same particular  challenge, I would say visualization is an appropriate tool too. People tend to  comprehend and get things faster when visuals are available.

input:
Mind Mapping - The case of a facilitated mobile selling platform.

Author: Yunier Leung

In 2011 a colleague and I embarked in a difﬁcult task, building the most advanced

craft beer e-commerce that our market had ever seen. Having worked together in the  largest craft beer company in Latin America, we had the experience and knew we could  do better. During the design process of the new business, we saw ourselves locked in a  sea of technical options on how the application could be built, what it would have and  what not. We collected about 200 features up to then, and they should be narrowed to a  maximum of a handful manageable items. But how?

The selection and application of the tool happened concomitantly. As we laid out so

many disconnected options in front of us, so we began organizing and connecting the  dots. Soon enough, the tool showed itself to us: Mind Mapping. It is extremely useful  exactly to that: surface patterns in a large knowledge base. The tool consists of  reorganizing the ideas hierarchically, starting from a initial, broad and general point. In our  case, this was the ﬁnal result: an app. Soon some clusters and a workﬂow surfaced, lot's  of correlated small pieces falling into larger ones, as steps of a larger construction.

After some days of work, the ﬁrst insight appeared: we just needed the steps really

useful to take products from our stock to the client, exactly how a well done mind map  should look, organized and sensible. Everything that stepped in the way of our goal should  and could be removed. We ditched away collecting too much information from our clients,  we built stocks near them, with really fast deliveries, removing the distance itself. Added  artiﬁcial intelligence algorithms to predict lot's of information that was not central to the  user experience, but that would facilitate the conversion. Lot's of features were  consolidated in small, really needed groups.

The approach turned out to be very efﬁcient. When we reached the prototype

version of the business it was elegant, simple and goal centric. In every new phase of the  business we reapplied designing mind maps so that everyone knew both processes,  hierarchies and what really matter: how everything connected. I could not change anything  beyond applying the tool earlier every time.
target:

input:
Challenge:

I was introduced to mind mapping through a mentor who challenged his employees to 'think different'.  When we met to work on a business problem one of the first things he would do is draw/doodle/map  what he knew and input from the meeting on a white board. I have also been part of mind mapping  exercises at sustainability conferences and in market research. Right now I am laid off late in my career  and looking for change towards doing something I will find interesting as well as provide a more  balanced lifestyle.

Selection:

So naturally the tool I selected is mind mapping. My intent was to think freely about what is important  to me combined with what I like to do within some type of goal framing to prepare for possible  execution of the idea.

Application:

First to prepare I read some books and articles. There was one from Fortune magazine with advice from  Warren Buffet about focusing on few things and prioritizing one goal in order to be successful. A  professor recommended the Marshall Goldsmith book Mojo. Also I was working with an outplacement  service to modernize my resume, practice elevator speeches and interview with recruiters to polish self  promotion skills.

While this was going on I set aside some quite time to mind map my immediate future. To think about  what I want life to be like combined with what I was learning. I took a large piece of paper and put 'Ashley  2015' in the middle. From there I thought about major areas of interest and spidered them out. The  areas I wound up with are: People, Health, Finances, Home. Each of these encompassed a few more  areas in detail so I kept spidering until I felt I had no more ideas to add to it.

Insight:

After leaving it alone for a day I sat down to review it. Overall I like having the visual to easily be able to  focus on how this years goals provide direction for the future. I found alignment in some of the items  and started drawing dotted lines between them. For example, journaling daily aligns with writing Linked  In posts and to my goal of writing a book about analytics.

Approach:

Mind mapping for goal planning is something that can be repeated as needed. There are apps for it that  I have used previously but I like just drawing it out with a pen and paper. It helps me keep focus on the  ideas rather than the technology. I don't think I would use a different tool. Rather I feel I would like to  continue applying mind mapping on this and other projects.

input:
FINAL ASSIGMENT IN DESIGN THINKING FOR INNOVATION.

I am Muhammad Shafiq and I joined healthcare industry in 2001.

I am going to discuss and share my experience in this assignment.

Challenge:

In one hospital we have introduced 4D ultrasound specifically for ANC patients.  Unfortunately we were failing to boost it as per our expectations and there was  no progression. Management decided to review the activities and planning to  redesign the marketing plan to attract target customers.

Tool selection:

(Referrence video : WEEK 4 Rossi)

We discover through survey that people are not aware about the benefits of what  we are delivering to them .So, we decided to create awareness workshop and  showing live images to them that they could understand.

For this activity we planned to use LEARNING LAUNCH Tool to achieve the goals.

We allocate one area near gynaecology department and giving handouts, lectures  and also first session of ULTRASOUND free of cost to show the difference  between traditional and 4Dultrasound. Also announced discounts on subsequent  visit in order to bring patients back to clinic.

Insights:

In this learning launch where we deliver ideal environment and deliver service to  customer results into tremendous increase in the number of patients and success  comes where patients visit in subsequent visit that could help in rating the  success rate.It also results in the morbidity rate of infants because of timely  treatment during pregnancy.  We got success and implement the same activity to help in promoting other  departments.

Aproach:(Visualiztion)

In future I would prefer to select Visualization tool because it is the best tool to  transform idea from mind to real world and the easiest and comprehensive way  to communicate whatever comes to your mind.

input:
Cristina Valerio

Design Tool: VISUALIZATION

Challenge & Selection

The tool I have decided to choose is Visualization, Visual Thinking as I prefer to call it.  I consider this tool can be helpful to count on the whole picture of the problem I am working on, in  the initial steps of the design thinking process. With it, I can support my effort to identify in the most  accurate basis, the pain point I intend to solve.

Visualization helps me to make the people pay attention to what is going on (What is?) by examining  what already exists. Visual thinking as a tool, guides my group, organizes it, leads the people to  discover the outcome desired.

As the first approach to the innovation process, this tool unlocks my thinking, unleashes creativity as  well as improves the way I communicate my ideas to the people I work with.

By using visual elements like charts and graphs, I provide an accessible way to see and understand  trends, outliers, and patterns in data and discover possibilities I have not seen yet.

Application

In my company, I am the manager of an area called Responsible Care. I usually work together with  my team, so this tool allows me to approach, identify, organize and communicate more effectively  the ideas within the group. The tool represents information visually, so it can be learned quickly and  easily.

I find it helpful to focus on the idea and guide my team members to the outcome I am looking for. By  this tool, I can communicate effectively the situation, and I challenge the people to explore the  problem, think possible scenarios and present prospect solutions for the outcome I am trying to  reach.

As I said above, I use charts and graphs, I can make a development of my concept and make it  visible for the group, the abstract idea becomes visible for them because it is expressed more  clearly.

Insight & Approach

With the knowledge I have gained throughout this course, I have imagined a better way of working  with my group, co-working all together to achieve more effectively the solution we are all looking for.

Cristina Valerio

new ways of working with visualization, adding posts to my initial diagram with ideas and  suggestions which arise from the discussions between the team members, grouping the ideas  according to the different criteria as a second step, and then finally vote for those which look more  representative of the solution we are seeking.

I recognize that the value added to the group by being aligned in the same direction, considering first  all the different ideas presented by each of the members, can strengthen us as a team, organize  information in a better way and keep all focused to achieve the results.

.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.

input:
Mind Mapping to identify Automation Opportunities

Design Thinking for Innovation by UVA Darden on Coursera

BY: Uwe Bas

21 Feb 2021

Challenge/Selection  I use the Mind Mapping often in defining a customer problem space when seeking to look at areas  where we can automate. Taking a customer centered approach to map out to the various stakeholders  and look at internal/external factors. Whenever presented with a customer problem I will build the  problem space. The challenges the team faces and mapping out how Automation can affect various  processes, value-chains and teams. It serves as a more engaging way than going through reports and  data. David Kelley, the founder of IDEO said, when I want to do something analytical, I make a list. When  I am trying to do something creative, I make a mind-map.

Application  I start the process by creating an overview of the value chain in the problem space and some select  focus areas based on understanding of the environment or other material. The understanding of the  environment is from a pre-work assessment and some interviews. Other material includes market  insights and our teams own automation expertise. The mind map is split into Above/Below the waterline  like a duck what the customer sees – Above the waterline vs what the customer does not see – Below  the waterline.

For the activity, we walk through the process at a high level and focus number of transactions and time  taken per transaction and high-level steps and applications used. After this initial walk-through, we do a  deeper dive on the key candidates looking at hand-off screens and data that is required to carry out the  activity. This allows us to exit the activity with a number of automation opportunity areas and some  candidates for automation.

Insights & Approach  What we have learned from these activities over the last 3 years is that people are engaged and they  openly share the challenges they see with the process. Also with the focus early on internal/external  factors, the team is able also identify areas of opportunity in other areas. We are always looking for  ways to improve how we carry out the exercises to drive better engagement. The team have built 100s  of automations and I have conducted 40 of these workshops. I approach this with a customer-centered  approach to reimagine tomorrow with automation.

input:
Design Thinking for innovation

Tool Applied : VISUALIZATION

challenge and Section

It’s almost impossible to tell if people are engaged or involved  with what you are presenting, that’s why i  consider visualization one of the most interesting concepts to put people in . In addition to that, visualisation  is really important for the process of design thinking, it is a way of unlocking a different part of our brain.  Since I adore programming , i found an interesting way to present my ideas , using the well know programing  languages, and espacially python where we found a library called Manim , which makes me able to animate  and illustrate pretty clear any idea i have in mind , and present it  in an interesting way to others .

Application

I start by making sure i understand the idea i want to present, and make even more sure that i am not going  to execute it in a linear way , when there is an easier way to do it.

After that , i write down all the ideas to be included in the animation (so i won’t get lost) , and start creating  the animation !

The first scene, is usualy a simple quote to illustrate the idea , which will be discussed, after that , i start  creating all the animations, I will create a different type of visual, either based on a high-level process flow  with swim lanes for different stakeholders, or another format.

For the activity, (while the animation is playing) i start asking question, it’s always import to make the  audience participate in the presentation, some of those  questions  :

1) What key elements are missing from the graphic?

2) What would you change about the graphic to make it more accurately reflect your problem space?

3) What key insights do you have about the relationships between the entities in your problem space?

I try to collect informations from the body language of the audience, I take the feedback and then recreate  the animation again , with respect to the valid points on their feedback.

Insight & Approach

What i learned from the session, The graphic gives the diverse group of stakeholders a point for discussion  that allows sharing their perspectives and developing insights into potential root causes of their problems,  it also gives time for reflection to the audience, and you make sure that they understood the message the  way you want it to be.I will always use the visualization activity as a starting point for a series of design  thinking .

By Lydia Wati

input:
Cynthia Chatel

Example Reflection – Visualization

Challenge and Selection

Visualization is used in my daily work life to communicate and organize thought  in different ways. Using visual thinking enhances the way we see things. Since  working at a publishing company, I use this approach with workers and clients.

During a project at work a client wanted to rearrange his entire branding  strategy one week before his launch, a project we were working on for almost  five weeks. We delivered the first draft of the project which he approved and  then a few weeks after he was unhappy, so we were tasked in redoing the  entire project over in less than a week.

Application

I began the entire process by first doodling his new changes, then we  compared it with other successful designs from this and other clients, comparing  feedback and feedforwards. Then we put all our ideas together on the drawing  board.

With this strategy the client was ultimately thrilled with the new plan, including  the new ideas that were included.

Insight and Approach

I believe we became overconfident with our first ideas on the project at the  time, and that I did not check in with the client until almost the last week. I have  now learned to always check in with clients on a weekly basis.
target:
⟨NAME⟩Cynthia Chatel⟨/NAME⟩

input:
Example Reflection - Storytelling - Jose Brown    Challenge & Selection  Storytelling can be a great communication tool to utilize. You can be  presented with a project or task and drill down to the actions items but if you  know the “why” behind the actions it can make all the difference in a  successful project.     I am in the healthcare industry and I work with IT on various projects and  enhancements. A recent project request from our OBGYN team to reduce  delays in getting critical report results for high risk pregnancies back to the  physician so they could take needed action. The current manual process  included faxing results and many times took hours or even a day before the  physician would see those results. This could add to an already risky situation.     This project seemed straightforward and the benefits obvious to everyone on  the team. But twelve months into the project and we could not get a  successful test completed. Many factors contributed to the stalemate. We did  not have a test environment to utilize and we had to use the production  environment to test. We experienced turnover in key roles, COVID 19 delays,  restricted physician availability and several other factors that added to the  delay.       Application  I opted to create a process flow to assist with testing so we could visualize  who was responsible for a specific task. We scheduled a meeting and opened  with a specific patient story that illustrated the risks these delayed reports can  result in without the change. Others on the call opened up about examples  they have experienced. We discussed the length of time this request has been  in process (many months now) and opened up the forum for ideas on how  best to tackle these delays. Many heads with valuable expertise are better  than my head and my limited experience with this report system. The visual  storyboard opened up the conversation and we are able to visually see how  many teams and individuals are involved in this process.

Insight & Approach  The results are still in the works. We are working through restricted schedules  with testers and physicians. But the team is more open and responsive and I  have a good feeling we will get this project completed much sooner than if we  had not included the “why” this change is so important and the impact it has  on our patients and internal customers.
target:

You can be  presented with a project or task and drill down to the actions items but if you  know the “why” behind the actions it can make all the difference in a  successful project.     I am in the healthcare industry and I work with IT on various projects and  enhancements. A recent project request from our OBGYN team to reduce  delays in getting critical report results for high risk pregnancies back to the  physician so they could take needed action. The current manual process  included faxing results and many times took hours or even a day before the  physician would see those results. This could add to an already risky situation.     This project seemed straightforward and the benefits obvious to everyone on  the team. But twelve months into the project and we could not get a  successful test completed. Many factors contributed to the stalemate. We did  not have a test environment to utilize and we had to use the production  environment to test. We experienced turnover in key roles, COVID 19 delays,  restricted physician availability and several other factors that added to the  delay.       Application  I opted to create a process flow to assist with testing so we could visualize  who was responsible for a specific task. We scheduled a meeting and opened  with a specific patient story that illustrated the risks these delayed reports can  result in without the change. Others on the call opened up about examples  they have experienced. We discussed the length of time this request has been  in process (many months now) and opened up the forum for ideas on how  best to tackle these delays. Many heads with valuable expertise are better  than my head and my limited experience with this report system. The visual  storyboard opened up the conversation and we are able to visually see how  many teams and individuals are involved in this process.

Insight & Approach  The results are still in the works. We are working through restricted schedules  with testers and physicians. But the team is more open and responsive and I  have a good feeling we will get this project completed much sooner than if we  had not included the “why” this change is so important and the impact it has  on our patients and internal customers.

input:
Design Thinking Course – Final Assignment - Visualization Tool – Learning Launch. Imtiyaz.

Challenge

I’m a member of the Digital Department of an international Company and my new challenge is  to implement a new tool in the Operations Department to improve the provider assignment  processes of their activity. The scope of the project consist in automatize the selection and  assignment of the providers of all the claims opened in the system. Agents (primary users)  often have problems with the IT tools because a lack of training or because they were not  involved in the building of the tool or understand the real benefit of it. The project duration is  estimated in 3 months and it includes the training session for all the stakeholders (agents and  providers).

Selection

We’ve decided to use visualization tools, starting with workflows to summarize the activity of  the assignment to jump to journey map and walkthrough to try the learning launch tool and  cover the main ressistances of the teams to the new it tools implemented in their activities.

Application

During the first two weeks we organize our first meetings we organize with agents and  providers to try to understand the high level process and depict it into basic workflows to  ensure the activity was clear.

After this sessions we spend 2 weeks to organize tours in the agents and providers offices to  see how is the daily activity and how their interact with the current tools.

Once the activity is clear, we went more in detail and we organize face to face meeting with  the agents and  providers to see how they manage their tasks, how they tackle their problems  and how they feel with the existing tools.

After that we present a journey map that represents their daily activity with the tasks,  problems and priorities in different scenarios to receive their feedback and test our  hypothesis.

The second month is centered in the build of the app and to present to the users wireframes of  the application as well as walkthrough of the navigation to ensure that all the activities, tasks  and suggestions are accomplished.

Insight

By applying this methodology both users (agents and providers) understood better their  activity and the benefits of implementing the new tool. It also provide them a wide vision of  the whole business and understand the problems of the others allowing a more empathic  behaviour between them. It also provide them a more sense of collaboration and satisfaction  after presenting during the last meetings the prototypes and the final solution to the rest of  the stakeholders and they started to think on how they can improve it.

Approach

Next time I would like to improve the organization of the interviews (with online solutions) to  increase the exploration involve more people in the co creation of solution that is key to  transform the activities and the organizations.

input:
REFLECTION ASSIGNMENT

Luis Vasquez

Design thinking

Design thinking its complicated procedure which aid sellers know what consumers  want and how to build contemporary solutions for different issues. It’s  multi-functional procedure which needs high talents and outlook best result in  contemporary ideas.

The Five parts of Design Thinking

Identify

Identification needs you to watch at things from the consumer’s point of view and

to link them psychologically. That is analysing in their way of thinking and their

perspective.

Determine the issues

In this procedure you collect all knowledge you collected during the identification

part and define the core issue and fix it.

Ideate

In this procedure it’s all about brainstorming meeting with your squad to find

remedy to the issue you gathered in the above process.

The provocation here is to imagine in different way and resolve this issue. There is

no blockade in brainstorming — on different ideas and creativity.

Model

The model serves to acknowledge which service and what doesn’t, and because

it’s a cheap cost version of our outcome and you can also make variety of versions

and upgrading the experience each time and to clarify our designs until you come

up with a perfect solution for our consumers.

Trail

Once we have upgraded our models with the reviews we received, we should

examine our final product.

examining shows if the issue we fixed is really solved. There should be a genuine,

noticeable variability in the behaviour or gratification of our consumers. You

should be prepared to fix the future issues if the solutions has some malfunction

or if does not work properly because the ultimate aim is to extremely understand

our consumers discomfort points and fix it with and proper successful  solution.

Conclusion

The ultimate aim of design thinking is to provide effective solution that fix real

issues and obtaining an satisfied results of the consumers, so there will be a high

chance of successful company and supreme product and service for long run of

the company.

Gracias

input:
Silvia Villalobos

Challenge:

There is a company which provides financial advisory to customers either in person or virtual.  Lately organisation climate has been seen decayed as result of arguments, hassles and the  lack of fraternity and cooperation among the campaign workers. The aim is improving  climate organisation to transmit unity and trust to our customer.

Selection:

Storytelling is the first tool selected, because this tool allows to connect with the audience,  to make understandable the message, and to transmit emotions. Campaign workers would  feel identified and understand the importance of tolerance and empathy.

Application and insight:

Many stories were told in various sections. Stories from previous experiences, fictional  stories, and stories told by participants created a different atmosphere due to participants  started to produce deeper relationships among them and recognize the importance of being  empathic. An important result was that meeting with clients were longer.

Approach:

The activities work in a good way, but visualization tool would work very well in a challenge  like this. Showing the way, some elements to get a harmonious atmosphere through  visualization is an option in a further situation.

input:
Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge

My name is Alberto and I’m a middle manager (one of many directors) of a Technology practice in a large  consulting company. I play the role of a project manager (or sometimes team lead) in the  implementation of complex corporate IT-systems, such as an ERP system or a corporate Data  Warehouse / Business Intelligence system. My clients are big companies, usually leaders on local  market. They work in different industries – oil & gas, metals & mining, coal production, power  generation and distribution, agriculture and so on, – but often have typical / similar problems and  requirements, so my experience is relevant to many of them.

I’m basically a technical (IT) specialist, dedicated to ‘delivery’ – system design and implementation. Sales  and marketing are not my favorite skills. But over the past three years I have been responsible for  developing an interesting complex functionality (Commodity Trading application) for a large coal  producer, and it makes sense to bring this accumulated experience to the market (to the C-level  management in the relevant companies).

Selection

Storytelling looks like the right technique for promotion of our skills and experience in CTRM  (Commodity Trade and Risk Management). My project team, together with the client, has come a long  way, made some mistakes (not fatal), learned many lessons and finally achieved good results, so I can  tell some interesting stories based on it.

Application

I have prepared two types of presentations about our project and CTRM experience:

•  Long story with a description of our client, business objectives, project approach, technical  architecture and many details and examples. It was addressed to a rather narrow audience – for  example, the participants of the metals & mining conference.

•  A more general and shorter presentation intended for a much wider audience– just description  of base problems, typical client requirements and issues, and outline of the possible system  architectures.

Insight & Approach

Then based on these materials (combination of detail comprehensive project description and kind of  elevator pitch about CTRM) I was ready for any scenario – long story for experts, short introduction for  newcomers and so on. But even very short story, to be effective, requires tones of background  preparation – analysis of target audience, development of the logical structure of the narrative, a  selection of striking examples from real life and … endless training.

input:
Storytelling  The Path to Innovation

Dr Sakir Ahmad

Challenge & Selection

Be it any organization, team or a government venture, I have often observed that they experience  failures because they couldn’t connect with their audience aptly. In such scenarios, storytelling  can turn the table significantly. I have used the tool ‘storytelling’ in my organization to convey a  complicated message as simply as possible or to generate ideas from people around me. The idea  is to implement this innovative tool to make the intricate issues understandable and engage the  targeted audience. I relayed the context of the story to captivate the attention of the audience. I  have used the technique in front of my team-members of the organization to impart its essence,  brainstorm notions, make productive connections and develop appropriate strategies. The crucial  element in storytelling is to engagingly answer the questions raised in the story to its targeted  audience and allow them to resonate with the core subject of the story. When I shared the story, I  tried to gain the insights from the audience as to how the characters inspire them and how it  related to their work emotions. The information and feedback immensely helped me in  recognizing its importance in finding solutions in real world.

Application

I used the storytelling tool in an organizational meeting to help the team members visualize the  essence and impact of revolutionary thinking. I understood that the story has to make sense so  that they can comprehend the context and relate with it. I started the story of the time when there  was an emergence of deadly diseases due to mosquitoes i.e. malaria etc. and a company brought  a revolution by introducing a range of mosquito repellent products. I emphasized on its out-of- the box thinking & ideas that made the brand conquer the market. I observed that my audience  were sincerely engaged and were emotionally connected with the company. After I finished, there  were questions and productive interactions which exhibited that they relate it with their ongoing  project. The enthusiasm of thinking afresh was quite overwhelming.

Insight & Approach

When I analysed the whole series of events it made me wonder, “Did my tool work? The answer I  think is ‘YES’. Will it serve the purpose? I think that time will tell but I believe it will certainly aid  them in making apt strategies.”

The exercise portrayed that a revolutionary story can influence a small number of people then  storytelling as a design-thinking tool, if implemented efficiently, can bring ground-breaking  changes in organizations & countries. I observed that my story engaged my targeted audience,  inspired them to think new and deliver better results.

As a student of design thinking, I can explore this tool from all corners and can confidently say  that I can creatively use it in number of inspiring ways at any appropriate phase of design thinking  activities.
