input:
Reflection

TOOL:  Visualization

Diego Campo

Challenge & Selection

I work as a bodywork quality engineer at an automobile assembly company. The factory where I  work has a low percentage of automation and the welding processes are manual by operators.  Because of this there are many quality problems associated with poor welding application. We have  resorted to all kinds of strategies to find root causes, we have changed machines, installed aids in  the stalls and nothing worked. At that time, we realized that we were not really understanding the  needs of welders for them to apply welding well. They also did not understand the reasons why we  made so many changes in the factory, there was no effective communication. At that time, we  decided to use the visualization tool. As we saw in the first week of the course, this tool allows us to  activate different areas of the brain to understand information differently.

Application

To better understand the difficulties and problems of the operators, we decided to create  personalized meeting spaces. We formed groups of 4 workers together with 2 engineers from our  quality problem solving team. The first step in the meetings was to ensure that we all understood  the quality defects that we had in the same way, for this we used visual tools such as photos of the  defects, videos showing incorrectly applied welding beads and some cartoons that showed the basic  rules to follow. The second step in the meeting was the opposite situation, now the operators taught  us visually in the workplaces the difficulties they had in doing their job well, each worker went to  his post, carried out the operation and taught us his problems so that together we could find a  solution. Using visual language, it was easier to understand the problems. We tried to apply these  solutions quickly to experiment and obtain the changes we needed.

Insight & Approach

That was the best strategy we could have used. With visualization we really understood where our  problems were coming from and the workers understood the basic rules for executing their duties.  After 2 weeks of holding these meetings we had already detected the positions where quality  defects and their root causes were generated. Using the same work teams, we generated alternative  solutions for these problems and implemented some while production was stopped. Some solutions  were successful on the first attempt, for others it was necessary to iterate until optimal solutions

were found. This exercise showed us the great power of visual tools to truly understand people's  needs and get to the root cause of problems. At this time, we have already removed more than half  of the quality defects that we had before using the tool.

Now in the other areas of the factory they have started to use this technique and I am sure that they  will also have excellent results.

input:
Example Reflection: Visualization.

Challenge and selection.

The first tool I used was the visualization of other team members, because it was

necessary to understand the needs of the people and have a common idea, and then I

was able to start planning possible solutions to the problems that could arise in the

development of the event.

The scope and limitations are other aspects that we want to develop, what kind of people

we will attract and how many people we can improve, so that people feel more

comfortable than when they compete.

Application.

Initially, the brainstorming process began, and then the information and information

gathered by customers' employees was used to create charts and sketches of the

company's new organization. This is very useful because customers are part of the

change, they allow us to improve and they warn us of the reasons for the failure of our

company.

We chose to add a template to this method to divide the customer's suppliers in order to

incorporate detailed company information so that we can work more efficiently.

Insight and Approach

What I have learned from this knowledge is to broaden my horizons and to be able to

see more clearly. Many tools that I think are temporarily not essential, but, on the

contrary, are essential for the correct development of thought. Sharing ideas is a good

way to create a unified environment, as other team members have the confidence to

participate and can analyze the data together to reach an agreement and specify the

most appropriate conditions for the company.

Ever since I discovered the visualization tool, I have realized that it cannot open its eyes,

but concentrates on daily work. Visualization allows us to open our minds to understand

the needs of those around us, so that we can use it to provide services that can benefit

them.

In another project, I will choose to use visualization tools, including storytelling tools,

because it is a good motivation for those who have not participated before to achieve the

results that can be obtained through all of these.

Camilo Lopez

input:
Example of Reflection - Value Chain Analysis – Jhonatan De Oliveira

Chalenge & Selection

I, as a business consultant, usually use “value chain analysis” to better understand the  routine of the organization I am studying, so I can identify what inputs are needed to  perform a given activity, what value is generated in that activity, and what the outputs  generated.

The reality of each organization is different, so it is important to carefully study each of  the realities and try to identify inputs to be able to improve the day of these companies  and consequently improve the impact on their customers.

Application

Recently I had the opportunity to apply Value Chain Analysis tool to a public health  organization in the city of Rio de Janeiro / Brazil. The main challenge was to understand  how the logistics process of the health supply chain works. In this way, the challenge  began with the purchase of medicines and ended with the delivery of these drugs in  specialized pharmacies.

During the study of the process it was possible to validate previously raised assumptions.  One of the biggest problems of the whole process was the delay and bureaucracy in the  purchasing process, more specifically in the bidding process. A bidding process could last  up to 360 days, which impacted directly on all pharmacies and consequently end users.

The process had the following major steps: ordering, purchasing, storage and  distribution.

Insight & Approach

With the in-depth analysis of the “value chain analysis” tool I was able to learn that  customer's "travel mapping" view is just as important when understanding only the  process of running the organization. To generate a better solution is necessary for the  customer, and you can do it.

The course of design that seems to me to show that it is necessary to see things as non- linear, always try to awaken creativity and listen as people. We cannot use data to  propose solutions to realities that are not known. You are looking for a product or  customer service.
target:

input:
Assignment Rubric - design thinking

Challenge & Selection

In 2009 my friend Luca came to me wanting to launch a brand of clothes that spoke about Maranhão (a region of northeastern Brazil). However, historically, there were only two brands that did very well in .  has a facility of exporting lifestyle and fashion for being one of the main postcards of Brazil. In Maranhão, the clothes that were manufactured were usually destined for tourists and did not have a personality. They were just "souvenirs" of the city. Knowing this I talked with Luca and suggested the application of some techniques of Design Thinking. Initially, we needed to understand the problem from the point of view of our consumer (empathy), and for this we began to look at which brands our target public most liked to use and interact with them to delve into the motives that made them buy these products. We realized that our target audience looked a lot like us and we consumed the same brands from other locations in Brazil. Knowing this we already had an idea of  how to start to validate the idea by doing a benchmarking with these brands that we consumed.

Application

As the goal was to launch a brand that spoke of Maranhao, it was natural to start talking about how Maranhão came about, who we were colonized, what people invaded us, and what heritage these people left us. After a very deep research on the history of Maranhão, the ﬁrst collection of the brand with prints of some postcards of the state appeared in stylized form, taking as references the type of design of the brands that we used and put to sell. It was a success!!!

Insight & Approach

With this we discovered that people loved to wear clothes that showed pride in being from the region and still did not do it because they did not have a more fashionable option. We took advantage of this insight and used this approach to bring our public even closer to the process of manufacturing clothes and stamping some features that only the people who lived in Maranhao knew and liked to be identiﬁed by such characteristics. In this way, the collections have a very strong word of mouth, without the need for large investments in advertising, because the customers themselves make a point of advertasing. Today I also work in a large shopping mall and will use the design thinking to redirect the way of communicating that the mall uses today. After this course, I learned some techniques and application steps of design thinking and especially the strength of storytelling to delight consumer. -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Última modiﬁcação:  21 de abr de 2019

input:
Reflection - Storytelling

Pietro Gabrielli

Challenge & Selection

In this course of Design Thinking I learned the basics about several design tools. One of those is storytelling. Storytelling is my favorite because it is so simple and easy to apply. It involves presenting your product or business in a story format that truly intrigues the potential customer or investor; while also creating a cause for a problem and making a list about different ways to solve it.

Application

Most likely, each and everyone of those reading this have gone for some shopping in some kind of clothing store and used a fitting room at least once in their lives. If you have used a fitting room several times, you must have felt that stress of not knowing when the employee might come knock on your door and open it because he couldn't hear you reply "taken" for some reason or another. Maybe the music inside the store was too loud, maybe you just panic and don't know what to do or say, or perhaps you happen to be deaf and/or mute. So, what happens next? The employee opens the door and there you are with barely any clothes on and some other customers catch a glimpse of you almost naked. If you go shopping regularly, then you must know the fear that I'm talking about.

While I was shopping myself I wondered: what if I were to design a device (either automatic or manual) that lets the employee know whether someone is currently using the fitting room or not?

Insight & Approach

My audience in this case, would be the customers that go shopping in clothing stores regularly, as well as investors and stores’ owners.

To apply this tool properly, it’s important to tell the story in the most intriguing way to the people that truly care about such things. After building a team for this presentation, we would need to be constantly asking relevant questions to our audience and then providing the answers to those questions. One of those questions could be: “How could this affect the overall customer’s experience apart from just using the fitting room?”. In that case, we can assure that the customer’s experience will be better, even if they don’t need to use the fitting room. Why? Because the employees’ workload will become less than usual, and that means they’ll be able to attend other tasks that are just as necessary, such as looking for a certain size for a customer, attending the cash register, welcoming customers into the store, and so on.
target:

input:
Design Thinking Assignment - Reflection

Dieter Berger

Challenge

My challenge is to convince people to reduce their organic waste in their houses

by descomposting it. We are developing an electrical domestic composter which is able

to reduce the organic waste, generated in the kitchen, in between 60% and 80% and it

takes no more than 72 hours. The compost generated by our device has got a lot of

nutrients and can be used for growing plants and trees. Our challenge is to create the

habit of decomposting organic waste in every house we can, and show that it can be

extremely important for our planet and vey handful to use on gardens and organic food

growers.

Selection

Storytelling is the chosen tool. It is able to cause a really good impact on the

people, helping them to visualize the importance and benefits of decomposting their

organic waste at home. By adressing them individually they will be able to feel that they

are really important in this process and they are trully a part of it. We want every

individual to be inspired through the current scenario, making them feel that they are

contributing for the environment’s future by reducing their waste, and in exchange they

can get the best nutrients for their plants and maybe grow their own food. The chosen

tool can help to make everyone feel and understand the purpose of the product.

Application

By using the storytelling tool we were able to tell our customers what is the real

impact of organic waste to our planet. Showing the percentages of organic waste

generated in the last decade by humans caused a huge impact and could make them

feel responsible for it too. Bringing this responsability to them and making them to feel

as part of it gave us a big chance to introduce our product and consequently the habit

of decomposting their organic waste at home. Some of them got really excited to be

parto f something with a bigger purpose, other enjoyed a lot the idea of producing their

own compost for their organic gardens. The story generated had a very positive impact

on their lives and was able to get mosto f the desired effect.

Insight

By applying the storytelling to the adressed problem I was able to feel part of

something bigger too. It caused a big impact on myself as well, making me feel

responsible to go further and help people with their organic waste too, showing them

that every human being must care about the waste they generate and that it has a big

impact to our planet. It came to me that I must work hard to make our product accessible

to everyone.

Approach

For the next time I would use storytelling with a visualization tool! By using both

of them I am sure I would have a bigger impact. By showing to their eyes what our waste

really cause to our planet I would certainly make them feel we are in trouble if we don’t

start reducing our waste now. Sometimes one picture is worth a Thousand words.

generated in the last decade by humans caused a huge impact and could make them

feel responsible for it too. Bringing this responsability to them and making them to feel

as part of it gave us a big chance to introduce our product and consequently the habit

of decomposting their organic waste at home. Some of them got really excited to be

parto f something with a bigger purpose, other enjoyed a lot the idea of producing their

own compost for their organic gardens. The story generated had a very positive impact

on their lives and was able to get mosto f the desired effect.

Insight

By applying the storytelling to the adressed problem I was able to feel part of

something bigger too. It caused a big impact on myself as well, making me feel

responsible to go further and help people with their organic waste too, showing them

that every human being must care about the waste they generate and that it has a big

impact to our planet. It came to me that I must work hard to make our product accessible

to everyone.

Approach

For the next time I would use storytelling with a visualization tool! By using both

of them I am sure I would have a bigger impact. By showing to their eyes what our waste

really cause to our planet I would certainly make them feel we are in trouble if we don’t

start reducing our waste now. Sometimes one picture is worth a Thousand words.

input:
Rustic Mango: Company General Use

Waliur Aslam – Initiating Data Helpdesk desk group

Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge & Selection

Storytelling is a great tool that I use it in my work. I work with Educatools, Exploration Data  Management division, Data Manager, and Group leader of Data Helpdesk. Many of them have a  challenge to control on the requests that we receive it from Exploration Organizations which has  multiple divisions and departments. I spend a lot of time to see how I can serve and support the team to  handle the remedy tickets. When I work with other groups, we share fact stories for different  reasons. Often they're interested to share stories to see how we can improve our life to improve the  business.  I have a story about using storytelling. In working with different entities, the group tried to meet and  explain about the situations that we are face it in the daily bases. The upper management assigned the  SME’s from each departments and division to measure the situation and report the minutes to improve  the business.

Insight & Approach

We listed all the comments and the feedback and we came back to our division to discover how we can  resolve the situation.

We worked with the Computer Center which is the focal contact and the focal point to serve all the  exploration organizations, we initiate an alias email that can the employees send their requests and the  data Helpdesk group will handle it and record it to be dispatch to the members in the division and  resolve the remedy ticket in proper way and reduce the resolution time to resolve it. After we  implement this role and deployed it in a simple we tool to be reported the daily outstanding remedy  tickets.

The assignee can consider and following up to their remedy tickets and they can be able to change the  status for each single ticket from assigned to in-progress and the requester will be notified that his/her  request is under process.

This course made me see this as a tool in a new way. When I've done it before it was really "ad hoc," but

now I can prepare ahead and think of stories that will assist me in presenting my ideas and best

illuminate them.

I have a design background, but I ended up as an entrepreneur in business. I had no idea this would ever

be considered an asset, but I am so very grateful that it is!

input:
Studio X

Roberto Hasan Papers

Empathy.Creativity.Design    1

paper #01 - Design Thinking

Roberto Hasan

Coursera – design thinking for innovation [university of virginia]

Storytelling

Challenge & Selection:

I choose storytelling because in my position as innovation-consultant I work with

different people, in different positions, with different circumstances and with absolute

different goals and challenges. I use storytelling regularly as a useful tool to clear

complicated situations and to narrowing divergent opinions from different parties. I

selected storytelling as my challenge because I was @ the beginning of a new start up

project and used it as a method to bring my idea to my audience

Application:

My storytelling-story is: When I started to create my idea for these absolutely incredible

organization, called studio x, nobody understands me. I had too many stakeholders who

are involved in the project and I had to explain my complex concept to each of them to

win them as my project-partner and to get the proof of concept for myself. Sadly just a

few people gave me an encourage feedback and I started to create doubt. Luckily, I

enrolled the coursera design thinking course and met some entrepreneurs who already

use storytelling successful in their business life. These things brought me in contact with

the method of design thinking, storytelling, …. So, I started to create success-stories for

my business-idea and introduced them to my audience and partner. The feedback was

amazing and the sadness when I told them that the project isn´t real @ the moment

too. While practicing telling stories to strangers I also built up some confidence and

more trust in my idea and vision, so it doesn´t just helps me to win peoples trust and

convince to invest in my start up, but also to bring the reason why I do this project in my

own mind every day.

Studio X

Roberto Hasan Papers

Empathy.Creativity.Design    2

Insight & Approach

From that point on the project started to get in shape and while creating my organization

I continuous used and use storys to clear my vision, the next step, the opportunities for a

single stakeholder-segment or just for me. As long I can inspire my audience with my

storys and they believe me nothing can go wrong and the path get cleared automatically.

I also did some research in this field and found out that storytelling is a powerfull tool

lead people and unite them behind a company mission, inspiring the organization,

setting a vision, teaching important lessons and define culture and values.

input:
Prepared By Ofelia

CHALLENGE & SELECTION

One of the most important tool and the relevant tool I learnt is  the visualization tool (i.e. the visual thinking) to manage my  stakeholders to understand the extend and depth of the pain  points we are trying to eliminate in our project. The use of diagrams like SIPOC diagram, ishikawa diagram and other sketches to display customers pain points during the onboarding  process which was manually done before we started the project.

Visualization is really important for the process of design  thinking because its actually a way of unblocking a different part  of our brain. It allows us to think in a non – verbal way and  access parts of the brain, our creativity. Most design thinkers call  visualization as “mother of all design tools”, because they are  used in every stage of the design thinking process.  Visualization  is equal part listening, thinking and drawing. Listening is the  input, thinking is the processing and drawing is the output. These  three skills work together equally in understanding complexity,  visualization outcomes to create new possibilities

APPLICATION

I start the process by creating a spaghetti mapping of our  customer onboarding process via in-branch and also linked all the  pain point using the ishikawa diagram and a pictorial description  of the stress level of different categories of the customer during  the onboarding process. The previous process was fraught with  high turn around time, decreased customer satisfaction.

The team was grouped into different group to visualize the  different customer segmentation and come up with ways of  increasing the customer satisfaction rate whilst increasing  revenue and customer acquisition. They use of the visualization tool helped to answer they 5 whys  questions used in the agile methodology and the outcome was  the build and use of an online onboarding process and also the  removal of pain points which are not relevant steps and the  reduction of the 14-steps process flow to  6-steps processes. This  input really impacted positively in the organization leading to  increase in customer acquisition of about 30%

BAD

INSIGHT AND APPROACH

What I learnt from using this tool as a solution package to  resolve customers complaint issues around the  onboarding process –is that visualization takes us beyond  just words or language alone. The beauty of using  visualization is that we can ta into a single picture. The  more you begin to use picture, the more you begin to  diagram while you talk and create a visual image for  people of how you are thinking. The team was able to  easily connect to the diagrams and visual displays and  easily wore the shoe of both the customer and an  innovative thinker to help resolve and optimize the  onboarding process.

Prepared By Ofelia

input:
MIND MAP

1. Challenge

Currently, my friends and I did a start-up about self-development applications.

Sometimes we found some difficulties in working together. For example, most of the

information comes in a non-organized, non-structured way, making it harder for me and

everyone else to absorb and utilize it. So, the way I’d phrase the challenge would be: how

can we improve our start-up by structuring information we handle in a simple, can be

visualized and knowing the relationship between that information that we have.

2. Selection

Our team uses a variety of design thinking tools, one of which is the Mind Map. Mind

Map is visually organized information that is designed to help us in collaboration and

communication.

3. Application

I use a mind map for structuring information to make it simple, can be visualized, and

knowing the relationship in a way I do organize the dozens of things that the team has to

coordinate. Every project basically contains the same items: scope (what’s in, what’s

out), timeline (dates, milestones), costs, resources (people, equipment), risks, and

assumptions. but, the hardest part of learning a new subject, is visualizing how big it is

and how its concepts and details are interconnected.

4. Insight

My insight about one of the design thinking tools, which is the mind map. I think by

making a mind map in the team, we have an idea of the performance we have been

through. In addition, we can also understand each other's ideas efficiently, the purpose of

the applications we develop is even clearer. Because the idea that we have is too big or

too complex for us to rely only on our memories. Here’s where the mind mapping helps a

lot.

5. Approach

As I am seeing the benefits of mind maps in our team, I definitely plan to continue using

the tool. But, I think that sometimes the map itself can oversimplify an idea because it

only contains a broad core not including important details. Overall, I do satisfy with the

mind map that helps me to simplify some ideas. Therefore, I confidently say that I would

MIND MAP

1. Challenge

Currently, my friends and I did a start-up about self-development applications.

Sometimes we found some difficulties in working together. For example, most of the

information comes in a non-organized, non-structured way, making it harder for me and

everyone else to absorb and utilize it. So, the way I’d phrase the challenge would be: how

can we improve our start-up by structuring information we handle in a simple, can be

visualized and knowing the relationship between that information that we have.

2. Selection

Our team uses a variety of design thinking tools, one of which is the Mind Map. Mind

Map is visually organized information that is designed to help us in collaboration and

communication.

3. Application

I use a mind map for structuring information to make it simple, can be visualized, and

knowing the relationship in a way I do organize the dozens of things that the team has to

coordinate. Every project basically contains the same items: scope (what’s in, what’s

out), timeline (dates, milestones), costs, resources (people, equipment), risks, and

assumptions. but, the hardest part of learning a new subject, is visualizing how big it is

and how its concepts and details are interconnected.

4. Insight

My insight about one of the design thinking tools, which is the mind map. I think by

making a mind map in the team, we have an idea of the performance we have been

through. In addition, we can also understand each other's ideas efficiently, the purpose of

the applications we develop is even clearer. Because the idea that we have is too big or

too complex for us to rely only on our memories. Here’s where the mind mapping helps a

lot.

5. Approach

As I am seeing the benefits of mind maps in our team, I definitely plan to continue using

the tool. But, I think that sometimes the map itself can oversimplify an idea because it

only contains a broad core not including important details. Overall, I do satisfy with the

mind map that helps me to simplify some ideas. Therefore, I confidently say that I would

like to explore more design thinking tools that will help me and my friend in developing

our small start-up.

input:
Maicol Toro

Reflection - Storytelling

Challenge & Selection:    I am currently working in a social service dedicated to helping women understand powerful  computer tools like coding. The girls I work with are usually from low-income families and do  not have access to many resources. For many of them, it is the very first time that they use the  tools we are teaching them about, so it is important for me to explain them in an easy and  comprehendible way. It is also important that I connect and build a relationship with them in  order to motivate them and to better understand what’s the best method to teach them. It is  important for me to use the storytelling tool in order to accomplish the goals I have because it  helps me understand the girls and create a cause for which to work towards together.    Application:    When I was first introduced to the girls, I applied the storytelling tool by telling them a story  about how the tools and resources I would teach them about had helped me solve many  problems throughout my life, I also gave them examples of how other people I know had used  the tools and resources to their advantage. I then talked with them and told them to tell me  ways they could use these tools and resources to their advantage, after this they were more  excited about learning to code and use the resources, we would provide to them. It got them  thinking about all the possibilities, once we got to work, they finished all their work and lessons  before the deadlines. They finished early and worked hard because I had connected with them  and they understood how this could help them later on.    Insight and Approach:    The challenge helped me learn how just connecting with people through stories can change  their whole approach to something and can greatly motivate them to work a lot harder because  it opens their eyes to how this is beneficial for them in the long run. By using the storytelling  tool, I was able to change the way they viewed the classes, instead of seeing it as a chore that  their parents and teachers forced them to do, they were able to see it as something that would  benefit them in the future. The next time I use the storytelling tool, I would change several  things. I learned that it is important to connect emotionally and logically with people and that  can mean talking about them, others or yourself. Next time I will have to be more conscious of  their response in order to see which stories are working and which are not.
target:
⟨NAME⟩Maicol Toro⟨/NAME⟩

input:
STORYTELLING

Paula Collins

1. Change and selection

Storytelling enables me to develop a deeper connection with the audience. I work with teachers  and I prepare  them in their permanente teacher’s training. I consider that storytelling is a  fundamental human experience that unites people and drives stronger, deeper connections. I  spend my time finding ways to inspire teachers. Sometimes they don’t want to participate in  courses because they have a lot things to do and they don´t earn enough money to participate  in courses but I use this strategy to motivate them to study as an inversion. I share stories of my  life in order to help them visualize how we can get success and get more incomes. I through my  story they connect and I can realize what are their needs for training. With this information I can  design courses according to their learning needs.

2. Application

I have a story about using storytelling. When I have to motivate teachers to participate in  teacher’s training courses I tell my story. I was a professor for 15 years at a university. I was a  teacher very recognized by my students. But one day I decided to study for a master's and  doctorate. There I met very successful professionals who presented their experience and work  throughout the country and the world. I lived in a small town and had never traveled to teach  at other institutions. One day, a friend invited me to give a conference with teachers. She invited  me to give a topic for an hour to 500 teachers and although I had never done it when I finished,  everyone applauded and the director of an institution of the capital of the country spoke to me  to prepare directors and teachers in the capital of the country.

In that moment, I realized the great impact that my presentation had caused. I went back to my  university and started looking for specialized courses in my profession..

3. Insight and approach

When I took these courses I met professionals from all the cities of the country and they invited  me to other events. At that moment, I realized that my best investment was to participate in  specialized courses of my career. Now I am a great recognized speaker in all of my country.

At first I thought that the time and money I invested would have no return, but it was the  opposite. Then I had to quit my job at the university and I am currently working giving lectures  and training teachers in my consulting company. I have my own schedule and it allows me to  give work to other teachers.

This story allows me to motivate other professionals to take the courses and to receive my  advice and then I invite them to participate as speakers in the congresses or consultancy that I  am in charge of.

This course help me a lot  to improve my job.

input:
VISUALIZATION REFLECTION

Challenge & Selection  As an 8th grade student, I use visualization a lot in my life. In my classes, I strive to not only memorize the information presented but also understand it. In order for me to do this, I use visualization. Some of my peers who struggle constantly ask me for aid in their respective subjects which we share. I wanted to challenge myself to only use the tool of visualization to help them. I selected visualization as we are now studying geometry and it's very important to learn how to visualize things.

Application  The main problems that my peers have with math are fairly simple to solve with visualization. For example, some of them aren't comfortable with the relatively important concept of isometries. In geometry, an isometry is a rigid transformation between metric spaces. Most of my classmates simply try to figure out and isometry by using a formula. However while being sloppy, a formula can give you inaccurate results due to an unforced error. Geometry, however, is the perfect candidate for usage of visualization.    What you have to remember while doing simple isometries is that it is usually possible to do without any formula what so ever. One example a 180 rotation transforms from (x,y) to (-x,-y). For people who were just barely paying attention in class, this concept isn't one they are likely to remember. So when I was doing homework with a friend and they start asking me for help on an isometry by saying they forgot the formula I switched my approach. In the past, I would simply tell them the formula. This time. I explained to them to visualize the shape than explained how it usually transforms diagonally. After doing this a couple of times I found it to be a lot more helpful for them and I switched my approach.    In this scenario, the tool was applied effectively. However, geometry is a very specific domain which visualization works very well. Most other areas of math cannot be explained this easily due to the fact they require complex formulas. Geometry worked very well because lots of geometry is a visualization so it is easy to expand that to other areas.

Fernando Soares

Insight and Approach  I've learned lots from this challenge specifically how to explain things to my peers in different ways. Apart from helping my peers, I can also help myself understand more concepts. Not only math but in all my subjects and also in life. Design thinking has really transformed my perspective on problem-solving. Now, when faced with a challenge or a problem I immediately turn to the steps introduced to me in this video.    If I were to do this challenge again I would turn to challenge a little more out of the norm to me. Geometry I felt was too easy to get a complete grasp on the concept I. Next time I would turn to a concept out of school and out of my comfort zone. Because I realized how powerful of a tool visualisation is I will use it now in my day to day life.
target:

input:
Reflection

Fabiana Braga 2012/09/11

Challenge and Selection The tool I selected to reflect about is the Learning Launch Tool. The purpose of a learning launch is to get the truth about a situation and a market, and that is useful so you can save time and money when to test ideas/hypothesis/assumptions.

To test ideas/hypothesis/assumptions, and verify if they are really good, it is important to compare with good data (and not opinions or unfounded guesses). That is a challenge due to some risks that are evolved in the process, as such as confirmation bias (when you subconsciously only look for data that confirms your idea/hypothesis/assumption).

Application I used the approach of the Learning Launch to test a hypothesis I have created recently. I work as a financial consultant in an Advisory Firm, and my job in the project that I’m currently involved is to create and suggest to my client KPIs for the cost process in the financial area. Therefore, I was supposed to bring some KPIs that could be used by managers to support their analysis of the development of the area.

To achieve this, I created a list of KPIs that could be used, and I tested the efficiency of the KPIs using the experiment phase of the Learning Launch Tool. This was important, because I could review the formulas I have created for the KPIs, and adjust them when necessarily.

Insight As I described before, I used the Learning Launch tool and I experimented the efficiency and reviewed the formulas for the KPIs. After that I could see the errors and adjustments necessarily to be done. By doing these steps, I was able to prove that the KPIs could be used by the managers, therefore I gained trust enough to finish the work.

Approach I would definitely use the Learning Launch again to experiment another idea/hypothesis/assumption that I have created. Also, next time I would use the Learning Launch as a starting point for my thinking and actions on a project.

To achieve this, I created a list of KPIs that could be used, and I tested the efficiency of the KPIs using the experiment phase of the Learning Launch Tool. This was important, because I could review the formulas I have created for the KPIs, and adjust them when necessarily.

Insight As I described before, I used the Learning Launch tool and I experimented the efficiency and reviewed the formulas for the KPIs. After that I could see the errors and adjustments necessarily to be done. By doing these steps, I was able to prove that the KPIs could be used by the managers, therefore I gained trust enough to finish the work.

Approach I would definitely use the Learning Launch again to experiment another idea/hypothesis/assumption that I have created. Also, next time I would use the Learning Launch as a starting point for my thinking and actions on a project.

input:
Reflection – Mind Mapping

Challenge

I am a member of the organization’s product development team. We are a multinational company from  the textile industry, and our challenge is to grow the sales of our line products. P&D works closely with  the Sales team, which brings us the feedback from our B2B customers, with our Trade Marketing  department, which brings us the feedback and information from the market, and the industrial  development team at the industrial plant.    The company spent a few months in a process of mind set and cultural change, when the employees  were trained in agile methodologies, to use them as new tools for the developments of the new product  process, and also solve problems in which the company has found difficulty.

Selection    After the training our team has already followed up with some parts of the "What Is" (Journey  Mapping, Value Chain Analysis), and now we have reached the stage of Mind Mapping, which is why I  choose this tool for reflection.    Application    First, we chose a large room in the office. Then we paste all the data that we collected during the  previous phases on the walls. We gathered a group of twenty people from the areas of sales, trade  marketing, product development and industrial, so they could look at it. Gradually everyone made  observations, and we followed the processes of insights formation and clustered the data so we could  proceed with brainstorming.    Insight    After the use of mind mapping, we could perceive a series of links and patterns among the interviewees,  and in this way, it was possible to observe some problems and feelings of the consumers that we didn’t  know before. Because of this process it was possible to reach the stage of brainstorming much more  prepared to develop a solution that fits the consumer better and adds more financial value to the  company.    Approach    For the next applications of mind mapping, I believe that what we will have different will be the  experience of having already carried out this process. All participants will already have changed their  mind set and understood the importance of design thinking, and the execution of this tool will already  be done in a more fluid way.

Lucas Moreira

input:
Design Thinking – Visualization

Kenichi Watanabe

Challenge & Selection:

The challenge I have in my organization is that we need to better manage our resources  time and allocation in the projects that the company has during the year and coming  years… The tool I used for this is Visualization or Visual Thinking to better understand  the problem between all IT team members in my department. The approach I used was  to first identity a way to how we could “predict” how much time each resource would be  working on specific projects during the next weeks. I started the brainstorming with an  empty excel spreadsheet where we “IT team members” would have to come up with  possible screens/fields that are needed in order to enter the work allocation for each  resource during the next weeks. This way we were able to create a “draft” template for  what a possible solution could look like to resolve our resource allocation in the projects.  The whole idea is to avoid that people are over-allocated and if they are working too  much, that creates other problems such as stress, quality issues, overtime costs, etc…

Application:

Once we had the initial “draft” excel spreadsheet created, we started the discussion  about how a possible solution could look like based on this template… We all got  together and started to think/imagine how the application should work, the possible  sequence, screens, fields, etc… and then we used a tool called “Bootstrap Studio” to  design the first prototype. This is one of the best ways to better visualize how the  solution could be created/developed, the steps, the screens, fields, behavior, user  interaction, etc…

We hard-coded all values for each step, screens, fields to better understand/visualize  what else could be missing, and finally we had the initial prototype created (without any  database integration)…

Insight & Approach:

When the initial prototype was complete, I learned that with this exercise with the team  members we could better understand the problem and we even came up with an initial  prototype of how the possible solution should be… We started to evaluate the options  about if we should develop our own software/solution for resource management or if we  should go with an existing option available in the market… After some weeks of  research, comparisons, discussions, a few trials, project costs vs benefits, etc… We  finally decided that an online solution which is pretty similar to the initial prototype would  fit the needs of our organization. We decided to go with a saas solution called “10000ft”  for resource management, and it fits pretty well our problem, and we are definitely  happy with the decision… 😊

eBooks Assistant

In this Design thinking project, I’m going to tell about my  project which name is eBooks assistant.  This is a website in  which there are many types of books are available and one  can also read any books in this site.

Now these are the features about this project.

➢ Nowadays, people are busy with their own busy

schedule. So this website helps to find books of their  choices easily.

➢ Also our website is user friendly, so one can easily

interact with it and can easily find its suitable book.

➢ This website is available in 3 languages.

➢ In this, one can find famous and popular books.

➢ In short our website is:-

• One device , many books

• Accessible Everywhere and for anyone

• Easily update

So these are all about my project. I also write some stories  regarding my project which are given below:

there was a boy named Sameh, a student of global school .  Once a book fest held in his school. So he participated in it. In  fest, everyone has to find best books.so raj also started for  searching books. He googled it but cannot find there. Then he  went to nearby libraries. he searched there also, but could not

find it. He became very frustrated and worried that he could  not get books and did not registered in fest. This is a sad story.

Now happy story regarding my project:

once in a school, the G.K. quiz was organized, so, Vikas  wanted to participate in it. By coming home, he went to  library to find it there. He visited another library but the  situation was same. Then he came in contact with one of his  friend. Then his friend said that “don’t worry about it because  I am already having a G.K. book.”  he gave his book to Vikas  then he happily participated in quiz. So these all about story  boarding regarding to my project.

There are several advantages of my project which are given  below:

Best books are available in this site.

Easy to read.

Motivation, general knowledge.

Some books are also free to read.

In short, my project is about:

This project is best way to understand a book.

The present situation is growing under future of a books

eBooks are becoming more widely available.

One can get motivation, general knowledge and skills  improvement and knowledge of historical book.

input:
Reflection – Storytelling

Diana Vazquez

Challenge & Selection

Storytelling in my field is a tool often used to connect with people all around the world who often  share an idealization of a dream or a story itself. Everyone has a dream, many people dream about  opening their own business themselves.

I have came across a lot of small business who were opened to the public with the main goal of  connecting with people and share their story (aside the obvious side of making a business). I find  these kind of processes very noticeable among the new trend of opening Little cafés or food  service places, people frequently visit these places no just because of the producto (food and  drinks), but because of the differential experience each of these business can give them.

Application

Nowadays people use social media to reach out to a broader public. It’s a very effective tool to  continue the path of storytelling itself. For example, i like to “hunt” for new places to visit every  time on social media, watching their Instagram feed can tell me so much more about the place  before i even get the opportunity to visit it, but it also makes my curiosity grow.

There’s a little patisserie that opened 3 months ago in the city, the owner used to make bread and  cakes for other cafés, quite famous around “the scene”, but she never got to open her own  business until recently. Al lof this was discovered by a simple approach and conversation, so I  wondered, how many people could feel motivated by her story and will her story make her own  brand seem more approachable and not just a single new business in town?

Insight

Using social media as a tool for storytelling can tell me so much not only about the place or their  producto, but their story itself. Has anyone tried to interview these entrepeneurs about the  challenges and dreams they had in order to get to that place were they finally were able to open  their business to public? How many of these stories can be relatable to the public itself? It makes  the whole experience more personal and engaging before you even have to visit the place, and we  know, not everyone has the time to have a 1 to 1 talk with the owner the first time they visit their  business.

Approach

Considering using an Instagram account to share the stories about these small business to the  people of a certain city or district. To chose this type of communication tool can make the content  available to a wider public and they don’t necessarily have to access a different website to get the  story. Owners can also share their story to their peers and the abundant circle of growing the  storytelling account can fuel the engagement.

This way, the next time a new costumer visits the place, they will have a familiar feeling lingering,  as if it’s visiting a new friend.

input:
Example Reflection – Visualization

Challenge

My name is Sunny, I am a civil engineering student and the challenge I was presented with  was given in the intrapreneurship course I was taking at the university which was about  how to inspire CORE@HTU's clients to use technological tools to optimize time in  transactions. In a group of 6 people we had to identify what were the key reasons why the  client did not access the digital platforms. The team focused on finding the main reasons  why the client did not use the digital platforms, and instead preferred to go to the  establishments in person. The deadline for the development of the pilot project was two  months and the solution had to present something innovative.

Selection and Application

The team focused on using the visualization tool as well as secondary tools such as the  customer journey or the empathy map. The main tool of visualization allowed us to the  main reason from the application of the 4 key questions: What is?, What if?, What wow?  and What work?  As it is mentioned in the first weeks of the course, it is from this tool  that the group can acquire a great number of perspectives since being 6 people,  considering that we were from different careers we could get the most out of the tool.

We also conducted research by acquiring first-hand information from customer  interviews, asking them basically what were the reasons why they did not perform  transactions using digital applications, and in the What is? stage, we were surprised that  one of the main reasons was that they felt insecure and untrained in the use of mobile  applications.

Insight and Approach

With all the information gathered from the first stages of the visualization tool, the team  was able to take advantage of auxiliary tools in order to complement the ideas. In the what  wow stage, we decided to create a different application, starting with the creation of a bot  that not only answered questions, but there was also the option for them to ask the bot to  help them throughout the process until they found what they needed. They told us about  the problem of insecurity, and from there we sought to implement the institution's  constant brand in the prototype, since we realized that it played a very important role in  making the transaction, since when the client saw that it was the institution to which the  transaction was being made, they did so with greater peace of mind.

Finally, we were able to learn that the solutions to our problems are not always in front  of our eyes but that we have to see a little further than that, we have to dig deep and see  the root of the problem, in that way and with the help of various tools we can look for the  main causes of our problems.

input:
John Estrada

December 6, 2021

Design Thinking Reflection

Mind Mapping

Challenge:  Creating a comprehensive multi-year strategic plan that captures new value- driven company goals and objectives that allow for business growth and  innovation is a difficult task. The level of complexity increases when prioritizing  which objectives offer the most value while minimizing risk; based on stakeholder  input, market data, employee feedback, and past lessons learned.

Selection:  To better understand the individual business components (gaps, past targets, key  performance indicators, etc) and align them with the collective stakeholder input  (data, key lessons etc) I selected mind mapping as my preferred tool.

Application:  Mind mapping was instrumental in my ideation sessions held over several days  with stakeholders across my company. Each session was 2 hrs long (3 sessions  total) with key stakeholders participating in all 3 sessions. The goal of my  ideation exercises was to:   1. Level 0: Start with the organization’s mission, vision, value at the center circle

2. Level 1: Itemize the various departments/business units that are parts of the  organization as a whole

3. Level 2: Create the following groups for each department: 1. Staff 2.  Technology 3. Process

4. Level 3: Create the following categories: 1. Challenges 2. Successes 3.  Future Goals then place stakeholder input, data, insight within each bucket

5. Draw connector lines for all dependencies and/or similarities between  departments

Insight:  Having the ability to visualize the various components of my organization and  showing how each department and their challenges, successes, and goals were  directly connected gave all stakeholders that participated in the exercise much  more clarity on the current-state as well as where they needed to be for the  future-state. The mind map made prioritizing the future roadmap less complicated  since all stakeholders were onboard. We all knew we were on the same bus and  finally had a map with a pin for our destination.

Approach:  I would not change my approach. Humans are visual creatures that identify  patterns naturally, that makes mind mapping an outstanding tool that can both  capture input from lots of stakeholders in real-time and connect all components  of a challenge at a 50k ft view down to a 1k ft view.

input:
Reflection: Visualization    Name: Paul Basha

Challenge   Recently I was the member of a team of 5 working on a problem statement of excelling in customer experience to help  home owners build a dream home. This problem statement was defined based on the reality of low foot fall and  conversion ratios in the engagement of home owners with interior designers. One among us in the team is an interior  designer by profession, while the rest of us have experiences as consumers at various points of time. The challenge was to  improve customer engagement/experience and thereby increase sales with a diverse consumer base – investors, first time  home owners willing to occupy the property themselves or multi property owners.     Selection   Visualization was a good tool to adopt to bring out clarity in our thinking and to prepare for a test launch/prototype. Visual  representation enabled better understanding & common ground on the challenges.    Application    After the problem statement was broadly articulated, we went through the 5Y method of identifying the possible causes  of the problem, followed by surveys/interviews of consumers. Patterns were identified and together with the insights from  the interviews, personas and empathy maps were created. We identified a particular scenario – “customer goes to  competition after the initial engagement” for ideation. Ideas were prioritized considering strategic importance and  difficulty of implementation. A visualization method was used to put forward the ideas for prototyping a glimpse of which  is in the picture below

Insight  The idea for the prototype revolved around ecommerce, mobility, collaborative ecosystem. The idea for the creation of a  collaborative ecosystem was seeded while applying the visualization tool. This idea got seeded because in an e-commerce  world it would be easier to bring in user experience that can be incentivised. This will be evaluated along with the  deployment of appropriate software platforms and tools. This phase also helped us in defining the problem statement  even better.     Approach   Visualization techniques were used scantily by us in the earlier phases of design thinking. On hindsight we could have used  lot more sketching during the ideation phase as well that would have increased the quality of our ideation.

input:
Kaur Rubio

Reflection: storytelling

Challenge

I am a university degree in administration, in the afternoons I work in a cafeteria  called "University ESAN" in which I have multiple functions such as customer service, food  preparation and charging; I have a colleague who has been working in the  company with which I distribute the tips for a longer time and in recent weeks I have  noticed that they have dropped and more on the days than on which I rest.

Selection

I chose the storytelling tool that is that connection between people and the  information that we share, use this tool since narration allows us to interact and tell  stories with which the two parties identify, that unite us and that lead us to a  common solution because as mentioned in video 2 called the storytelling tool  “storytelling allows you to create energy, passion and emotion about something“  and I think the most important thing is to find the way in which a story helps us to give  it strength the other.

Application and Insight

One day at work I dedicated myself to observing my colleague and noticed that  when she attended to clients she did it in a rude way so I took some time with her  and we started talking, she told me that she was very strained first because she was  under pressure. through school and secondly because she did not have the support  of her parents, with that I could understand her and I told her that I also studied and  like she was very stressed by all the homework they left me, but that I went to a  psychologist who He allowed me to realize that this was the cause of my bad mood  with others because it expressed my frustration, but with his help I managed to  organize my work and student life and with it my frustration diminished.

Later she told me that she is going to a psychologist who is helping her a lot and for  my part I try to talk to her, but more importantly to listen to her, be empathetic and  find support and identification with me in order to see that everything has a solution.

Approach

This tool allowed me to see the cause of the problem and I can even say that it  made me more empathetic by being able to understand what was happening. I  can affirm that the implementation of the tool benefited us because in the following  weeks we have seen an increase in our tips and my partner looks happier, but what  could change is that this was not only between the two but also expanded it with  my boss as one motivation towards everyone, where the entire work circle comes  together and we flow together.
target:
⟨NAME⟩Kaur Rubio⟨/NAME⟩

DESIGN THINKING COURSE  Suman Salim – Universidad San Francisco de Quito Honduras  Reflection Asignment

1. Tool: MIND MAPPING

2. Challenge:

Implementatio of biosefaty measures for the new incoporation to face-to-face  academic activity.

3. Selection:

It will allow to clarify ideas in a simple way, organize and connect ideas visually.   I can include a brainstorming with my team and discuss the ideas in a group, so I can  organize the information.

4. Application:

  We identify the needs

  Each member of the team made contributions to each need

  We locate the ideas of the contributions according to each need

  We made the connections to identify priorities

  We define the actions according to the prioritized needs

  We prepare a work plan for implementation

5. Insight:

  We were able to represent the ideas, concepts and tasks, linking it to our main  project.

  We link the different elements contributed by each team member, using them as  key points to address specific ideas.

  We develop the ideas starting from the important project point, according to the  objective of defining the main actions to be implemented for the implementation  of the necessary measures for the incorporation of face-to-face academic activity  on campus and headquarters.

  We achieve greater clarity in the identification of needs and actions relevant to the  project.

  We organize information in a better way.

  It allowed us to better plan the activities to be implemented in a work plan.

6. Approach:

Define more clearly the scope of the project in stages, to identify the needs and  required actions.

DESIGN THINKING ACTIVITY - 4

Project: Electric bikes

Team Number:  05

Team members: Karan Patel

TASK 1 - STORY TELLING IN PROBLEM FRAMING

Given below are some of the problems expressed by the

consumers

1. E-bikes tend to have low resale value.

2. Maintenance and repairs are too costly.

3. Riding range remains low.

4. Battery charge time is too long

5. Battery has a rather short life span

TASK 2 - STORY TELLING IN SOLUTION FRAMING

Technical Details:

Security:

 Air Bags for saving the life.

 Can be connected to smartphones.

 Tube less tires to avoid punchers in between of a journey.

 Adjustable height according to their convenience.

 It has gears as normal bikes with different power levels.

Tracking:

 It can be tracking through mobile in a smartphone manner

 So that we can check the status of the bike charging,

condition etc.

Battery Life:

 It has a good battery life that lasts more than the existing

bikes which makes you travel more distance.

 Capacity of the battery is 65V 30Ah

Performance:

 The acceleration rate is 20kmph in a second

 The brakes are disc brakes that acts instantly.

 The charging time is 5 hrs

 The battery lasts for 70 kms

Solutions given by designers to the

consumer problems:

1. Running Costs: As they are expensive as compared to

petrol bikes but when we think about the fuel and

electricity it will say that it wasn’t more expensive.

2. Motor Life: It can be used more than 8 years with running

25000kms without any single issue.

3. Resale Value: The resale value of E-Bike is less compared

to fuel bike but it is more friendly to the current generation

of people.

4. Battery Life: The battery life is good but you have to be

aware of some safety measures of the parts in E-Bike like

not making the bike over charging.

Advantages of E-Bike both Technically

and Financially:

 It is eco friendly that helps us to save the environment that

the present generation is wanted to do.

 It saves the wastage of money compared to fuel bikes as

the price of fuel is more now a days  Consumer Feedback:

 The seating is comfortable and easy to balancing. The height

problem is resolved for the short raiders.

 The E-Bike’s tracking system and airbag system helps to

monitor our vehicle and safety of the raider.

 The vehicle is smart and smooth to handle. Its battery is also

last good enough to a good ride. It is affordable to buy.

 I am glad to have product like this.

Coursera Challenge

MINDMAP

AGILE Implementation Challenges as a design thinking Mindmap

Student- Shujon Rohman

The Assignment Rubric

This Coursera assignment depicts the usage of Mindmapping as a tool for design thinking for a problem – AGILE Implementation Challenges

Core Challenges in

Implementing AGILE in a

Matrix Organization

Challenge –

Implementation of AGILE

practices in a Matrix

organization

Rational – Misconceptions

about AGILE are prevalent

in a traditional

organization. Employees

have different mind

blockages. This exercise

helps to understand those

and come up with a

strategy

Selection – In our highly

matrixed organization,

with traditional IT,

changing mindset is

difficult

Application – We did this

exercise to understand the

key concerns, like adapting

to change, availability of

business for quick POC and

so on. It was a session with

business and IT leaders

Insight – Every

participant were asked to

state the issue with AGILE

manifesto, it was mapped

to central idea.

How we Did it? –

Assimilate the central and

tertiary ideas and map

them. Look at

correlations and concerns

and address them

Wastage of Money

Long Term

MINDMAP- AGILE implementation Challenges

Being AGILE

What is

What WOWS

What if

What Works

The Outcome

Mindmapping lead the team to understand the issues and come up with a solution approach

01

WHAT IS

02

WHAT IF

03

WHAT WOWS START

04

WHAT WORKS

WHAT IS

In this part the teams mind mapped the main

issues. The central of that being business was not

accepting change, which was due to risk in the

well established ways of working, inducing more

cost and time to perform the work.

WHAT IF

In this section we presented the team with a

comparison in a brainstorming session as to what

will it look like if we had new softwaresvwhich

allowed to save time and money. Also the

business did a competitive analysis of where they

are falling behind in the market and come up with

a roadmap.

WHAT WOWS

While immediate changes to softwares and

upgrades seemed a great idea along with

automation, but disrupting the business had

customer impacts leading to legal and

compliance impacts

WHAT WORKS

A systematic planned replacement / upgrade of

the softwares, with proper OCM along with a IT

and Business joint team to look at each

implementation problem will help getting the

teams on board. Also leadership level change was

desired

and come up with a

strategy

Selection – In our highly

matrixed organization,

with traditional IT,

changing mindset is

difficult

Application – We did this

exercise to understand the

key concerns, like adapting

to change, availability of

business for quick POC and

so on. It was a session with

business and IT leaders

Insight – Every

participant were asked to

state the issue with AGILE

manifesto, it was mapped

to central idea.

How we Did it? –

Assimilate the central and

tertiary ideas and map

them. Look at

correlations and concerns

and address them

Wastage of Money

Long Term

MINDMAP- AGILE implementation Challenges

Being AGILE

What is

What WOWS

What if

What Works

The Outcome

Mindmapping lead the team to understand the issues and come up with a solution approach

WHAT WORKS

WHAT IS

In this part the teams mind mapped the main

issues. The central of that being business was not

accepting change, which was due to risk in the

well established ways of working, inducing more

cost and time to perform the work.

WHAT IF

In this section we presented the team with a

comparison in a brainstorming session as to what

will it look like if we had new softwaresvwhich

allowed to save time and money. Also the

business did a competitive analysis of where they

are falling behind in the market and come up with

a roadmap.

WHAT WOWS

While immediate changes to softwares and

upgrades seemed a great idea along with

automation, but disrupting the business had

customer impacts leading to legal and

compliance impacts

WHAT WORKS

A systematic planned replacement / upgrade of

the softwares, with proper OCM along with a IT

and Business joint team to look at each

implementation problem will help getting the

teams on board. Also leadership level change was

desired

difficulties in using it. For example, branch got to complicated and too

However, it is still very useful and handy to use.

2.  Selection:

Just as what it is sound like. Mind map is an effective tool to map your

mind. To put your and your colleges mind into one piece of work. It is

very useful to brain storm using this tool.

complicated and too much. Or on the other time it is hard to use it as a

objective and bias view. However, it is still very useful and handy to use.

4. Approach:

I would confidently say that I would love to explore in using other tools.

Just as this tool, hopefully other tools might help me in developing this

small start up ideas. It is better for you to know a lot of tools but still

exceling in some particular part. On this case I would still use Mind Map

as my core tools in storming out ideas.

Nurul Ghani

input:
Pablo Romero Example Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge

My challenge was that I had to found this new society on creativity, leadership, innovation and design.

Therefore, I had to convince people in my college that it was a good idea and I would gain their

participation with this way. So, long story short, I had to influence people along the foundation of this

creative society. Among with that, there were other stakeholders such as society’s collaborative

companies and college management.

Selection

When it comes to affecting and influencing people under your organization, in my opinion, there is no

better choice then storytelling. I have a story to tell them about why the existing societies are not as

good as they should be, and there is no society that would excel students’ soft skills. If you want to

create, you need to create. You cannot just listen to people who create and then become a creator

yourself. This idea led to turn my thoughts to a story then to tell people. Some say storytelling have

four P’s: People, Place, Plot, and Purpose. Therefore, I chose storytelling to be able to show these

aspects.

Application

Before assembling people together for the board of this society, I have took them one by one and spoke

privately with each. Emotional branding is the key point in here for society and I have tried that. Each

individual was from different disciplines, because this society is a multidisciplinary one. Hence, I aimed

for their needs and how this society can have benefits for their faculty as well. Apart from that, our

collaborative companies had their needs as well. After all, it must be a win-win relationship and I told

our story according to that. I explained the benefits of cooperation with telling stories. People had to

chance to show empathy with this storytelling applications and were all eager to participate in it.

Insight

Applying this tool really showed me that systematic and emotional approach when it comes to using

storytelling works wonderful. Showing empathy to people’s needs and influencing them using design

thinking has many benefits. Apart from that I gained experience with my story each time I told it then

each time I did better at telling it.

Approach

I would apply this tool for a similar case because I saw that people needs to start with “why?” instead

of what. In addition, I was able to create complexity in their heads and then solve it by answering with

storytelling.

Another tools like visualization and mind mapping is actually applied with this method too but they

weren’t directly. I might consider using mind mapping more directly too because mind map is a diagram

used to visually organize information. A mind map is hierarchical and shows relationships among pieces

of the whole. Which would benefit my explanation of the society to other people.
